gemini.text: Queries are being interpolated twice when using shard-based splitting because runSplitQuery now performs interpolation and runShardSplitQuery also performs interpolation before calling runSplitQuery, causing inefficiency
Need to add a skipInterpolation flag to QuerySplittingOptions interface to allow runShardSplitQuery to call runSplitQuery without triggering a second interpolation
The call to runSplitQuery in shardQuerySplitting.ts needs to pass skipInterpolation: true to prevent double interpolation

qodo.text: datasource.applyTemplateVariables may mutate the passed query object, which can unintentionally mutate request.targets for the remainder of the request lifecycle. Consider ensuring applyTemplateVariables operates on a clone, or clone the query before passing it in.

The filter order differs between split paths: one path filters by expr before filtering out hidden queries. It's safer/clearer to consistently drop hidden queries first to avoid running templating/interpolation work on hidden queries.

The new applyTemplateVariables mocks mutate and return the same LokiQuery object instead of returning a new object. This can hide real-world issues around immutability and make call-count expectations brittle. Prefer returning a new object (e.g., { ...query, expr: ... }).

bugbot.text: When shard splitting is used with ad-hoc filters, applyTemplateVariables is called twice on the same queries: first in runShardSplitQuery at the start, then again in runSplitQuery when the shard splitting logic delegates to it. Since the subRequest spreads the original request (preserving request.filters), ad-hoc filters get applied twice via addAdHocFilters, resulting in duplicate labels in the final query expression like {job="test", namespace="default", namespace="default"}

copilot.text: Inconsistent filter order: The filters are applied in a different order compared to querySplitting.ts (lines 296-298), which filters by !query.hide first, then by query.expr. Both files should use the same filter order for consistency.

Double interpolation when runShardSplitQuery calls runSplitQuery: queries are interpolated in runShardSplitQuery, then interpolated again in runSplitQuery at line 299. This can cause issues with adhoc filters being applied twice or nested variable references. Fix by adding a skipInterpolation flag to QuerySplittingOptions.

Test mock mutates input query object instead of returning a new object like the real applyTemplateVariables method does (using object spread). This discrepancy could hide bugs related to object mutation.

Second test mock also mutates input query object instead of returning a new object like the real applyTemplateVariables method does. Should return a new object with spread operator to match real implementation behavior.

propel.text: In shardQuerySplitting.ts, filtering by query.expr before applyTemplateVariables means targets whose expressions become empty after templating will still be scheduled and sent to Loki, resulting in invalid/empty queries. Templating should be applied before filtering.

In querySplitting.ts, filtering by query.expr before applyTemplateVariables means targets that become empty after templating will still be executed and can produce malformed requests. Templating should be applied before filtering.

baz.text: runSplitQuery pre-interpolates with datasource.applyTemplateVariables, but datasource.runQuery still calls DataSourceWithBackend.query which applies the same interpolation/addAdHocFilters again before hitting the backend, causing ad hoc filters to be appended/escaped twice for every split request

runShardSplitQuery calls applyTemplateVariables before computing shards, but applyTemplateVariables intentionally leaves $__range/$__auto untouched. When splitQueriesByStreamShard later runs getSelectorForShardValues it sees selectors still containing those literals, so fetchLabelValues uses an invalid stream selector and never discovers shards for queries that reference $__range/$__auto in matchers, silently breaking sharding for those queries

coderabbit.text: Call-count expectation in test at line 112 expects 5 calls to datasource.applyTemplateVariables, but runShardSplitQuery only calls applyTemplateVariables once per initial target (this test has one target), so the expectation should be changed to 1

kg.text: Logic bug in querySplitting.ts - filtering by query.expr before interpolation means template variables that resolve to empty strings won't be filtered out. The filter should be moved after applyTemplateVariables

Logic bug in shardQuerySplitting.ts - filtering by query.expr before interpolation means template variables that resolve to empty strings won't be filtered out. The filter should be moved after applyTemplateVariables

qodo-v2.text: Shard splitting repeats template variable interpolation multiple times: once in runShardSplitQuery(), again in runSplitQuery(), and again during execution via DataSourceWithBackend.query(), causing unnecessary CPU overhead and potentially exacerbating ad-hoc filter duplication issues

devin.text: Double interpolation when shard splitting delegates to time splitting causes duplicate ad-hoc filters - runShardSplitQuery interpolates queries via applyTemplateVariables, then passes already-interpolated targets into runSplitQuery which calls applyTemplateVariables again, resulting in addAdHocFilters being called twice and duplicate filter labels being added to queries sent to Loki

greptile-v4.text: Double interpolation of ad-hoc filters in shard-split path: runShardSplitQuery applies applyTemplateVariables at line 52, but splitQueriesByStreamShard internally calls runSplitQuery which also applies applyTemplateVariables, causing addAdHocFilters to be invoked twice on each query when request.filters is non-empty, producing duplicate filter conditions in the final query

The fix should either clear request.filters on the subRequest passed to runSplitQuery since ad-hoc filters have already been applied, or runSplitQuery should only apply applyTemplateVariables when it is the entry point (not when called from runShardSplitQuery)

Test assertion only validates call count (expect(datasource.applyTemplateVariables).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(5)) but does not verify correctness of final query expressions - should check for no duplicated ad-hoc filter labels and correct step values
Missing test variant that uses non-empty request.filters to guard against the duplicate ad-hoc filter regression

mra-max.text: Loki applyTemplateVariables performs backend interpolation using scopedVars to replace variables via templateSrv and applies ad-hoc filters

Interpolation-before-splitting breaks range-variable gating because interpolating beforehand replaces $__range, so the guard may no longer detect range-variable usage, allowing splitting for queries that originally included $__range
runShardSplitQuery now uses applyTemplateVariables instead of interpolateVariablesInQueries, affecting how queries are templated prior to splitting
groupTargetsByQueryType returns a Promise but errors aren't propagated to the Observable, causing potential silent failure from unhandled promise rejection
Shard values parsed from label values may yield NaN, leading to invalid shard arrays and disrupted shard-based grouping logic
Tests verify expr and step interpolation but not legendFormat interpolation in runSplitQuery
Inaccurate inline doc referencing non-existent runNonSplitRequest() fallback in shardQuerySplitting.ts header comment

mra-nano.text: Double interpolation risk in Loki split query path: runSplitQuery applies applyTemplateVariables per-query inside the splitting logic, while DataSourceWithBackend may also interpolate via interpolateVariablesInQueries or applyTemplateVariables downstream, creating risk if shouldApplyTemplateVariables remains true for Loki queries

Interpolation migrated to applyTemplateVariables inside both split paths (runSplitQuery and runShardSplitQuery) rather than upfront in LokiDatasource.query, representing a structural behavioral shift that increases regression risk if applyTemplateVariables semantics diverge from interpolateVariablesInQueries

Mutation risk in interpolation path: applyTemplateVariables() returns a new LokiQuery object, but test mocks mutate the input query directly, and if production code or mocks mutate the passed object, it could mutate targets in request.targets leading to inconsistent queries across shards

Test fragility from new interpolation contract: tests in querySplitting.test.ts and shardQuerySplitting.test.ts assert applyTemplateVariables invocation counts and interpolation behavior, making them brittle if interpolation semantics or query counts change

mra-ultra.text: Circular dependency between LokiDatasource, querySplitting, and shardQuerySplitting modules - LokiDatasource imports runSplitQuery from querySplitting.ts while querySplitting.ts and shardQuerySplitting.ts both import LokiDatasource, causing potential undefined exports during module initialization

Unguarded scopedVars/filters passed to applyTemplateVariables in runSplitQuery() and runShardSplitQuery() without null/undefined guards, while test request objects are constructed without these fields, risking runtime errors
Unhandled promise rejection in runShardSplitQuery - groupTargetsByQueryType Promise has no .catch() handler, so if it rejects the error is not propagated and can crash or hang the observable stream

applyTemplateVariables mock/call-site argument count mismatch - test mocks accept only one argument while real call sites pass three (query, scopedVars, filters), and some production call sites pass only two arguments

mra-b.text: applyTemplateVariables not defined on LokiDatasource — runSplitQuery and runShardSplitQuery call datasource.applyTemplateVariables(), but LokiDatasource only defines interpolateVariablesInQueries, causing runtime errors and test/mock mismatches

Query splitting check can be bypassed by early template interpolation — applyTemplateVariables() replaces template tokens before querySupportsSplitting checks for $__range, causing isQueryWithRangeVariable to fail to detect the original variable and incorrectly allow splitting of queries that should not be split

Early interpolation may incorrectly substitute backend-deferred variables like $__auto — applyTemplateVariables intentionally excludes certain scoped vars from interpolation so the backend handles them, but test mocks permissively replace them, hiding potential differences in real behavior and query semantics

mra-a.text: applyTemplateVariables method is not implemented on LokiDatasource, causing TypeError at runtime while tests pass because they mock the method directly

Circular dependency between querySplitting.ts and datasource.ts - querySplitting.ts imports LokiDatasource from datasource.ts while datasource.ts imports runSplitQuery from querySplitting.ts, which can lead to partially initialized modules and runtime errors

Interpolating variables before deciding whether to split may break $__range detection - if applyTemplateVariables expands $__range before querySupportsSplitting runs, the isQueryWithRangeVariable check will fail to detect it, causing queries that should not be split to be incorrectly split

sourcery.text: Filtering on query.expr before applying template variables causes queries whose expr is empty until templating (e.g., built entirely from a variable) to be dropped before reaching splitQueriesByStreamShard - should apply template variables before filtering on expr

In runShardSplitQuery and runSplitQuery, mapping over request.targets and passing each query into applyTemplateVariables without cloning may mutate the original request.targets if applyTemplateVariables mutates its argument - should clone query objects before applying templates

The assertion expect(datasource.applyTemplateVariables).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(5) in shardQuerySplitting.test.ts couples the test to exact internal call counts - should assert on transformed queries passed to runQuery instead to focus on observable behavior

cubic-dev.text: Stores interpolated targets that runQuery() interpolates again, so split queries can get ad hoc filters appended twice
request.filters is passed to applyTemplateVariables but the test helper createRequest never sets up filters on the request, meaning ad-hoc filter application is untested in the query-splitting path

mesa.text: Switching from batch interpolation (interpolateVariablesInQueries) to per-query interpolation (applyTemplateVariables) may change multi-value variable expansion and Loki-specific escaping behavior, especially for complex variable types like custom or regex variables

Missing test case for interval variables ($__auto, $step) relative to split time windows - since interpolation now happens before splitting, these will use global/initial values rather than per-split values, which should be explicitly tested

Interval-dependent variables ($__auto, $step) are now interpolated globally before splits are calculated rather than per-split, which may not match intended behavior and could break queries expecting per-split interval values

Duplicated filter-then-interpolate pattern across split execution paths (runSplitQuery and runShardSplitQuery) creates maintenance risk - preprocessing logic should be extracted into a shared helper to prevent future behavioral drift

codeant.text: Mutation of original query objects: applyTemplateVariables mutates the passed query, which means request.targets array will be mutated before splitting happens, potentially causing unexpected side effects for callers expecting request.targets to remain unchanged

applyTemplateVariables signature inconsistency: code calls datasource.applyTemplateVariables with different arguments (query, scopedVars, filters) in some places but only (query) in others, which may cause typing mismatch or runtime errors
Deprecation/removal of interpolateVariablesInQueries: need to ensure the function is removed or kept but not used, and other parts of the codebase using it should be updated

Test fragility with exact call counts: tests expecting applyTemplateVariables to be called exactly 5 times may break if internal splitting behavior changes; should assert on call args rather than exact counts

Edge case handling for query.hide and empty expr: if applyTemplateVariables could set hide or clear expr, a query with template-only placeholder that resolves to empty might produce empty expr after mapping but still proceed
Inconsistency between runSplitQuery and runShardSplitQuery: both interpolate first but may pass different arguments to applyTemplateVariables (some pass scopedVars and filters, others just query)
Missing TypeScript types and documentation: LokiDatasource needs updated types for applyTemplateVariables with appropriate params and return type, plus JSDoc describing mutation behavior

kodus.text: Redundant interpolation creates invalid queries - runShardSplitQuery already interpolates template variables before calling runSplitQuery, causing double interpolation that duplicates filter expressions when ad-hoc filters are used, causing query execution to fail

State mutation in test mock - the mock implementation for applyTemplateVariables directly mutates the expr property of the input query object, which causes the replace operation to only succeed on the first call when the function is called multiple times with the same object

The querySupportsSplitting guard for $__range queries is defeated by pre-interpolation - after applyTemplateVariables is called, $__range is replaced with a concrete value, so isQueryWithRangeVariable no longer detects it and queries containing $__range are incorrectly routed into the splitting path

claude-code.text: The new applyTemplateVariables call in runSplitQuery (line 298) causes ad-hoc filters to be duplicated on every split query, because DataSourceWithBackend.query() also calls applyTemplateVariables downstream, and addAdHocFilters unconditionally appends filter clauses without deduplication
For shard-split queries, applyTemplateVariables is called three times (runShardSplitQuery + runSplitQuery + DataSourceWithBackend.query), tripling the ad-hoc filters
The fix should remove the applyTemplateVariables call from runSplitQuery and runShardSplitQuery, relying solely on the existing call in DataSourceWithBackend.query()

Tests don't catch this bug because they mock datasource.runQuery so super.query() and its applyTemplateVariables call never execute, and the mock applyTemplateVariables does an idempotent string replace that hides the duplication

qodo-v2-2.text: Shard filters multiplied - runShardSplitQuery() now pre-applies applyTemplateVariables(..., request.filters) and then calls runSplitQuery() per shard group, which again pre-applies applyTemplateVariables(..., request.filters) before executing via datasource.runQuery() (which applies it again). cubic-v2.text: Interpolate before filtering `query.expr`, otherwise template-only queries that resolve to an empty expression can reach the shard-splitting path and crash `runSplitQuery()`

bugbot.text: The tagDeviceUI function adds the device to the local cache (line 87) before calling CreateOrUpdateDevice (line 93). When ErrDeviceLimitReached is returned because the device limit is reached and the device doesn't exist, the cache entry remains. On the next request, the cache hit at line 83-84 causes an early return with no error, bypassing the database check entirely. This allows blocked devices to authenticate successfully on retry within the 29-minute cache TTL, defeating the device limit feature.

The constant 30 * 24 * time.Hour representing the anonymous device expiration period is now defined in three separate files: anonymousDeviceExpiration in database.go, anonymousDeviceExpiration in api/api.go, and thirtyDays in impl.go. This PR adds a new duplicate in database.go instead of consolidating into a single exported constant. If the expiration period ever changes, it would need to be updated in multiple places, increasing maintenance burden and risk of inconsistency.

In updateDevice, returning ErrDeviceLimitReached when rowsAffected == 0 conflates multiple cases (nonexistent device, expired device, concurrent update); consider distinguishing these or renaming the error/adding logging

In CreateOrUpdateDevice, the time range for CountDevices and updateDevice is recomputed with separate time.Now() calls, which may lead to subtle off-by-a-bit inconsistencies; capture now := time.Now().UTC() once and reuse it

cubic-dev.text: Missing type annotation causes TypeScript to infer the type as `undefined` instead of `number | undefined` (as declared in the `GrafanaConfig` interface). This prevents assigning a number to this property through type-checked code paths.

Returning the error here is correct for device-limit enforcement, but `tagDeviceUI` sets the local cache *before* calling `CreateOrUpdateDevice`. When the store returns `ErrDeviceLimitReached`, the device key is already cached, so subsequent requests within the 29-minute cache window will get a cache hit, return `nil`, and bypass the limit. Consider clearing the cache entry on error in `tagDeviceUI`, or moving `SetDefault` after the successful store call.

When the device limit is reached, new (previously unseen) anonymous devices receive `ErrDeviceLimitReached` from `CreateOrUpdateDevice`. If the upstream authentication handler treats this as a hard failure, anonymous access is completely blocked for new users rather than gracefully degrading (e.g., allowing access but skipping device tracking). Consider returning a sentinel error that the caller can handle non-fatally, or handle the limit-reached case without returning an error.

Race condition: the count check and the subsequent insert are not atomic. Multiple concurrent requests can each read a count below the limit, pass the check, and all proceed to insert—exceeding the device limit. Consider using a database-level transaction with an appropriate isolation level, or an atomic conditional insert (e.g., `INSERT ... WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) ...) < limit`), to enforce the limit atomically.

No validation for negative `device_limit` values. A negative value silently disables the limit (treated as unlimited) because the downstream check is `deviceLimit > 0`. Consider clamping or rejecting negative values to avoid silent misconfiguration.

The count query in `CreateOrUpdateDevice` calls `time.Now().UTC()` twice (for `from` and `to` bounds), which can return slightly different instants. More critically, if the database stores timestamps in server-local time (common with MySQL/SQLite) while the code sends UTC values, the BETWEEN comparisons may not match the intended device window. Consider capturing `time.Now().UTC()` once in a local variable and ensuring consistent timezone handling with the database layer.

`rowsAffected == 0` does not necessarily mean the device limit was reached. The UPDATE could match zero rows because the device doesn't exist yet (new anonymous user), or because the device's `updated_at` falls outside the BETWEEN window. Returning `ErrDeviceLimitReached` for all these cases is misleading. Consider differentiating between "device not found" and "limit enforced" to avoid confusing error handling upstream.

The query string is wrapped in `[]interface{}` and then splatted into `dbSession.Exec(args...)`. While this mirrors an existing pattern in `CreateOrUpdateDevice`, confirm that `Exec` correctly handles an `interface{}`-wrapped string as the SQL argument across all supported database dialects. Additionally, the `?` placeholders in the UPDATE query may not be automatically translated for PostgreSQL, which expects `$1, $2, ...`—unlike the dialect-specific queries in `CreateOrUpdateDevice`.

Test naming inconsistency: the other tests in this file follow the `TestIntegrationAnonStore_<Method>` convention. Consider renaming to `TestIntegrationAnonStore_BeyondDeviceLimit` or similar to keep the file consistent.

qodo.text: I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! is executed on every request in set_locale, which may introduce unnecessary per-request work and potentially repeated filesystem loads depending on caching. Consider ensuring fallbacks are loaded once per locale change or at boot.

FallbackLocaleList depends on mutable global state (SiteSetting.default_locale, I18n.locale) and is assigned to global I18n.fallbacks. Under multi-threaded servers where I18n.locale is per-thread but I18n.fallbacks is shared, ensure_loaded! may load/modify shared structures unsafely.

The ensure_loaded! method tracks loaded locales via @loaded_locales which is mutated without synchronization. Two threads may attempt to load the same locale simultaneously, or @loaded_locales may be observed in a partially updated state. Consider adding a lock around locale loading and the set update.

bugbot.text: The FallbackLocaleList#[] method mixes string and symbol locale types in the fallback chain. The locale parameter is typically a string (from I18n.locale), while SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym and :en are symbols. Since Ruby treats "en" and :en as different values, uniq won't deduplicate them, causing the same locale to be processed twice in ensure_loaded! when a user's locale matches the default

copilot.text: ensure_loaded! method reads and potentially modifies @loaded_locales without proper synchronization, bypassing LOAD_MUTEX protection used in load_locale, leading to race conditions in multi-threaded environments

FallbackLocaleList#[] method calls SiteSetting.default_locale.to_sym without checking if the setting is available, which could raise an error during application initialization before site settings are loaded

ensure_loaded! method iterates over I18n.locale and its fallbacks, but I18n.locale may not be set at the time the initializer runs, causing issues if accessed before I18n.locale is properly initialized

The comment 'order: after 02-freedom_patches.rb' does not follow Rails convention for controlling initializer load order since Rails loads initializers alphabetically by filename - consider using a numbered prefix like '03-i18n.rb'
The call to I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! assumes I18n.fallbacks is always set and available, which will raise NoMethodError if I18n.fallbacks is nil

augment.text: Calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! in set_locale only makes fallbacks work for controller requests, but jobs/mailers that use I18n may not pass through set_locale and therefore may still not have fallback locales loaded

Changing the site default locale at runtime could change fallback results, but TranslateAccelerator caches I18n.translate by config.locale only, which might keep serving cached fallback strings from the previous default locale until I18n.reload!/cache reset occurs

@loaded_locales tracking is sensitive to whether callers pass locales as strings vs symbols (e.g., 'en' vs :en), which can lead to duplicate entries and repeated loads - should normalize locale to a single type before checking/adding

propel.text: Fallback locales are only loaded from ApplicationController#set_locale, so background jobs, mailers, and rake tasks never load fallback locales (SiteSetting.default_locale or :en), causing I18n::MissingTranslation errors even when translations exist. The fix is to load fallback locales when constructing the fallback list in FallbackLocaleList#[]

Calling I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded! only in ApplicationController means fallback locales are never loaded for non-controller codepaths (mailers, Sidekiq jobs, rake tasks). The loading logic should be moved into the I18n layer itself (e.g., in the translate hook or inside FallbackLocaleList#[]) so every translation call preloads the fallback chain regardless of execution context

cubic-dev.text: The `locale` parameter is not normalized (e.g., via `.to_sym`), so passing `"en"` vs `:en` would bypass the `include?` guard and load the same locale twice. The existing `translate` method always uses `config.locale` (a symbol), but `ensure_loaded!` is a new public entry point with no such guarantee.

The lazy initialization `@loaded_locales ||= []` is not protected by `LOAD_MUTEX`, unlike every other mutation of `@loaded_locales` in this class. In a multi-threaded server this can race with `reload!`, which resets `@loaded_locales` and `@cache` in sequence. Wrapping the check-and-load inside `LOAD_MUTEX.synchronize` (similar to `load_locale`) would make this safe.

mesa.text: The `ensure_loaded!` method iterates through the fallback chain calling `I18n.ensure_loaded!`, but there's no explicit check that this method exists on the I18n module. This appears to be calling back into the monkey-patched method in `translate_accelerator.rb`. Consider making this dependency more explicit or adding a guard to prevent failures if the patch isn't loaded.

The `@loaded_locales` instance variable is not thread-safe. In multi-threaded servers (Puma in production), concurrent requests could race when checking and modifying this array, potentially causing duplicate locale loads or partial visibility of loaded locales. Consider using a `Concurrent::Set` or wrapping access in a `Mutex` to ensure thread safety.

The custom `FallbackLocaleList` replaces Rails' default fallback behavior entirely. This increases maintenance burden and could break across i18n gem upgrades. Consider adding comprehensive tests covering: regional locale variants (e.g., pt-BR -> pt), plugin-provided locales, and edge cases with missing/invalid locales to ensure this custom implementation remains robust.

The fallback chain accesses `SiteSetting.default_locale` on every lookup, which may not be available during early initialization (before database connection or settings are loaded). If `set_locale` or translation occurs before SiteSetting is ready, this will raise an error. Consider adding a safe default or ensuring SiteSetting initialization order is documented.

This assumes all translation usage occurs after `set_locale` runs. If any middleware, error handlers, or early controller concerns use I18n.t before this point, they won't have fallbacks loaded. Consider either: (1) calling `I18n.fallbacks.ensure_loaded!` in a before-boot initializer as a safety net, or (2) documenting that this must run before any translation usage.

cubic-v2.text: `locale` is not normalized to a Symbol (or String) before the `include?` check. Consider adding `locale = locale.to_sym` at the top of the method.

`@loaded_locales ||= []` is not protected by `LOAD_MUTEX`, introducing a race condition. Two threads could simultaneously see `nil`, each create a new array, and one write is lost. The fixture, pretender route, or requested id should be adjusted to align

WHERE s.name = 'embed_category'` and `SELECT value FROM site_settings WHERE name = 'uncategorized_category_id'` always return at least one row, but if either setting is missing or `embed_category` doesn't match an existing category name, `execute(...)[0]` will return `nil` and calling `['id']`/`['value']` on it will raise, breaking the migration. Admin::EmbeddableHostsController#update and #destroy use .where(...).first and then call methods on the result without handling nil, so invalid IDs or race-deleted records cause 500s instead of 404s

CreateEmbeddableHosts migration can crash by indexing empty SQL results with [0], aborting deploys when expected site_settings rows are missing

CreateEmbeddableHosts migration inserts raw embeddable_hosts strings directly into the new table without escaping or normalization, so previously-allowed values like http://example.com/path can be migrated into records that will never match EmbeddableHost.record_for_host host lookups

qodo-v22.text: Migration assumes embed_category row exists and indexes [0] on an empty SQL result when the legacy embed_category setting isn't set, causing the migration to raise and abort deploy/boot before the fallback to uncategorized can be reached
Host strings are interpolated directly into INSERT SQL statement without escaping, so a host containing quotes can break the migration or be interpreted as additional SQL
EmbeddableHost runs sub! on self.host in before_validation without guarding nil, so a missing host raises NoMethodError instead of producing a validation error
Admin::EmbeddableHostsController update/destroy does not handle a missing record (where(...).first can return nil) and then calls methods on nil, returning 500 instead of 404

After saving an embeddable host, the UI sets host.category using the pre-save categoryId rather than the server-persisted category_id, causing client/server state mismatch when server defaults a blank category to uncategorized
The embeddable-host component does not catch errors from destroyRecord(), so a failed delete results in no user-visible error and an unhandled promise rejection

qodo-extended.text: EmbeddableHost runs sub! on self.host in a before_validation callback without guarding for nil, so creating/updating a record with a missing host raises NoMethodError instead of returning a validation error
Admin::EmbeddableHostsController update/destroy do not handle missing records and will call methods on nil (save_host(nil) or nil.destroy), causing 500 errors instead of a 404/JSON error response

The migration copies the old embeddable_hosts setting into the new table by interpolating host strings directly into SQL, so quotes or unexpected characters in the stored setting can break the migration or inject SQL

The migration unconditionally accesses [0]['id'] on the embed_category query result. On fresh installs where this setting doesn't exist, the query returns zero rows, [0] returns nil, and nil['id'] raises NoMethodError, blocking all migrations

In _hydrateEmbedded, when a property matching the _ids pattern has a null/undefined value instead of an array, obj[k].map() throws a TypeError. The singular _id path has a null guard but the plural _ids path does not

type.replace('_', '-') in JavaScript's String.prototype.replace with a string argument only replaces the first occurrence. Any admin model type name with multiple underscores would fail to match its hyphenated entry in ADMIN_MODELS, causing incorrect non-admin path resolution

cubic-v2.text: `self.host.sub!` will raise `NoMethodError` if `host` is `nil`. The `before_validation` callback runs before the format validation, so a nil host bypasses the validates_format_of check and crashes instead of producing a validation error. Guard with a nil check or use the safe navigation operator.
Fetch the singleton embedding record by id instead of calling `store.find` without an id, which resolves to a collection.
Guard the `embed_category` lookup before indexing it. As written, missing data raises before the uncategorized fallback can run.
The query lowercases the DB column with `lower(host)` but does not downcase the input parameter. Call `.downcase` on `host` so the comparison is consistent.

qodo-extended-summary.text: The :embeddable_host fabricator is defined in spec/fabricators/category_fabricator.rb but depends on the :category fabricator which was moved to a different file; because spec_helper requires fabricator files in filename order, the suite can fail to boot with an undefined fabricator error

In Admin::EmbeddableHostsController, both update and destroy call .first which returns nil for non-existent IDs, then immediately invoke methods on the result without a nil guard, causing NoMethodError (500 error) instead of a proper 404 response

The database migration assumes that embed_category exists in the site_settings table, but instances that never modified it will return an empty result, causing execute(...)[0] to be nil, and nil['id'] will raise a NoMethodError, crashing the migration

The migration interpolates legacy host strings directly into an INSERT SQL statement without quoting/escaping; a host containing a single quote can break the migration and the pattern is vulnerable to SQL injection

In _hydrateEmbedded, the plural _ids branch unconditionally deletes the original ID array and replaces it with hydrated results even when _lookupSubType returns undefined for some or all IDs, unlike the singular _id branch which guards deletion on successful hydration

type.replace('_', '-') in basePath uses JavaScript's String.replace with a string argument, which only replaces the first underscore occurrence; any admin model type with multiple underscores would fail to match its hyphenated entry in ADMIN_MODELS

The before_validation callback in EmbeddableHost calls sub! directly on self.host, but if an API request omits the host parameter, self.host is nil, and nil.sub! raises a NoMethodError resulting in a 500 Server Error instead of a 422 Unprocessable Entity

mra-max.text: Global color function migration from scale-color to dark-light-choose creates high churn risk of visual regressions and merge conflicts, plus potential compile issues if variables are missing in some themes
Widespread changes to high-PR files (admin_base.scss, headers, topic lists, topic posts) amplify risk that a single theming tweak or bug will ripple through large portion of UI
In admin_base.scss, h3 color for .setting.overridden uses scale-color($highlight, $lightness: -50%) bypassing dark-light-choose pattern, creating potential contrast issues in dark themes
admin_base.scss has multiple scale-color usages not wrapped by dark-light-choose (lines 418, 443, 496), breaking dark-mode support consistency
Typo/syntax error in topic-list SCSS patch shows truncated 'color: dark-ligh' which would cause compile-time SCSS error
In _topic-list.scss line 115, color uses scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%) without dark-light-choose wrapper, inconsistent with surrounding usage
Header color rule at line 301 uses dark-light-choose with primary as light-theme color, contradicting goal of dark-theme colors relying on $secondary
Header color rule at line 332 reuses primary in dark-light-choose call, risking dark-theme deriving from primary rather than secondary

Centralized dark-light-choose function creates tight coupling between theming palette and all UI components - changes to primary/secondary colors or brightness logic will cascade across thousands of style rules
Large-scale color refactor in desktop topic-list styling may lead to inconsistent theming across breakpoints if secondary color not defined identically in all themes
Mobile topic list styling updates add churn across mobile styles with potential for regressions in mobile rendering
No explicit color-contrast tests or visual regression tests across light and dark themes despite hundreds of components relying on new color logic
Broad patch scope touching desktop, mobile, and admin styles increases surface area for regressions
dark-light-choose function relies on theme_variables imports - if import graph changes or theme_variables unavailable in some build contexts, builds could fail
PR description is minimal ('Test 7') and does not summarize changes, rationale, affected files, or testing performed
No CHANGELOG.md present despite project referencing standard changelog practices - change affecting theming/tokens should have changelog entry
Partial adoption with some color usages remaining as direct tokens (e.g., color: $primary) creates mixed color strategies within same theme
Static uses of $header_primary in topic-post.scss not part of dark-theme toggle via dark-light-choose, making dark-theme behavior potentially inconsistent
No dark-theme-specific tests found in test folder covering dark-theme behavior or fallback logic
No visual regression or theming tests that would guard the CSS theming changes introduced by this PR

mra-ultra.text: dark-light-choose function relies on global color vars ($primary/$secondary) for brightness decisions, which could produce unexpected results if files override these variables in nested scopes
Inconsistent lightness distributions across color replacements (80%/20%, 75%/25%, 50%/50%, 60%/40%) may lead to uneven contrast and readability between elements depending on theme
Remaining scale-color usage in topic-list (.category .badge-notification color) not migrated to dark-light-choose pattern used elsewhere
No unit tests or visual regression tests validating dark-light-choose color outputs for light vs dark themes
Outdated inline doc comment in discourse.scss claims default table cell color uses scale-color($primary, $lightness: 50%) but actual code uses dark-light-choose with different lightness values

Large set of callers updated to use dark-light-choose() need review to ensure correct argument ordering matches the function signature (light-then-dark vs dark-then-light)
Large-scope mechanical CSS replacement across many frequently-edited stylesheet files creates merge-conflict and regression risk
Heatmap/coldmap classes now use dark-light-choose() which may unintentionally change numeric highlight semantics by swapping color source to $secondary based on brightness heuristic
In mobile/modal.scss, .custom-message-length color change uses 30% lightness for primary instead of original 70%, which differs from other similar changes and may be a mistake

mra-a.text: dark-light-choose function has inverted logic - returns $light-theme-result when brightness($primary) < brightness($secondary), which is the dark-theme condition, causing wrong colors in dark themes

Automated refactor swapped lightness values inconsistently across desktop/mobile - multiple selectors have primary-branch lightness values changed (e.g. 30%→70% or 70%→30%) and desktop vs mobile counterparts for the same element use inverted lightness pairs

Existing call sites pass reversed args to dark-light-choose (secondary, primary) to compensate for current semantics, but PR's bulk replacement uses (primary, secondary) order without preserving that compensation, creating inconsistent behavior across stylesheets

qodo.text: Potential DoS via unbounded pagination: the limit param in GroupsController#members is client-controlled and not capped, which could allow very large queries/responses
Pagination math off-by-one: totalPages uses Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1, which yields an extra page when user_count is an exact multiple of limit
Pagination bug: next caps offset to user_count, which can produce an empty page (valid last offset should usually be user_count - limit)
members accepts limit directly from params and applies it to the query without a server-side max, allowing clients to request very large limits increasing DB load and response size
add_members splits a comma-separated string but does not trim whitespace, dedupe, or handle empty tokens, which can cause unnecessary lookups and surprising behavior
remove_member deletes by user_id directly instead of using the same membership API as group.add/remove, potentially causing callbacks/validation consistency issues

bugbot.text: Pagination total pages calculation uses Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 which calculates one extra page when user_count is a multiple of limit - should use Math.ceil(user_count / limit) instead. This causes the next button to remain enabled on the actual last page, allowing navigation to an empty page.

The remove_member method calls group.users.delete(user_id) with an integer ID, but the delete method on a has_many :through association expects a User record object, not an integer. This will fail to actually remove the user from the group.

copilot.text: totalPages is computed with Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1, which over-counts when user_count is an exact multiple of limit (e.g., 50 users @ limit 50 reports 2 pages). Use Math.ceil(user_count / limit) for correct pagination.

next sets offset to min(offset + limit, user_count), which can move the offset past the last valid page start and produce an empty result set. Clamp to the last page start (e.g., max(user_count - limit, 0)) to avoid navigating to empty pages.

create forces group.visible to false whenever the param is omitted (nil == "true"), overriding the model default (visible is true by default). Only assign visible when the param is present to avoid new groups becoming invisible.
create ignores alias_level even though the client sends it, so alias level is not persisted.

update unconditionally sets group.visible based on a string comparison; if visible is omitted from the request it will be reset to false. Only update visible when the param key is present to avoid unintended visibility changes from partial updates.

add_members uses group.add(user) (which persists the join row immediately) and then calls group.save to decide success. If group.save fails, the endpoint can return an error even though memberships were already added. Persist membership changes atomically instead.

remove_member uses group.users.delete(user_id) on a has_many :through, which deletes join rows without running GroupUser destroy callbacks, so the user_count counter_cache can become incorrect. Use group.group_users.where(user_id: user_id).destroy_all instead.
findMembers returns undefined when name is empty (return ;), but callers sometimes treat it like a promise. Return a resolved promise consistently.

The offset clamping in findMembers allows offset === user_count (and the controller's next action also clamps to user_count), which yields an empty page and breaks page count math. Clamp offset to the highest valid page start.
Database query operation happens inside a loop and could be hoisted to a single call outside the loop for better performance.

augment.text: totalPages uses Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1, which over-counts when user_count is an exact multiple of limit (e.g. 50/50 -> 2 pages), allowing next to set offset to user_count, yielding an empty last page and incorrect currentPage/totalPages display

The automatic variable may not be in scope for the item view template - if it resolves against the member context it may be undefined, causing the remove control to show even for automatic groups, which would allow clicking remove and likely surface a 422 without UI handling

offset = params[:offset].to_i can produce negative offsets and limit can be <= 0, which may raise or produce invalid queries in ActiveRecord - need to clamp/sanitize limit and offset before calling .limit(...).offset(...)
Spec uses xhr :put for remove_member but the route is defined as DELETE /admin/groups/:group_id/members - using PUT can let the spec pass while the real route/verb would behave differently

propel.text: The add/remove member routes are defined outside of a `member` block, so the generated paths are `/admin/groups/members` and do not include a group id, causing 404 errors when the Ember client calls `/admin/groups/${id}/members.json`

In add_members, the code raises ActionController::ParameterMissing because the Ember client only supplies the group id in the path and no `group_id` parameter in the request body - should read from params[:id] falling back to :group_id
In remove_member, same issue as add_members - the client never sends a `group_id` parameter, only the id embedded in the URL, causing ActionController::ParameterMissing and 400 errors

When user_count is an exact multiple of the page size, Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 reports one extra page, keeping the next button enabled even when there is no data - should use Math.ceil instead

The user selector sends comma-separated usernames with spaces after commas, but the server looks up usernames verbatim, causing User.find_by_username to return nil and silently skip members - should strip whitespace and skip blank entries

baz.text: alias_level from the flat payload is never assigned before group.save, so creating a group with a non-default alias_level from the UI silently drops that value
totalPages uses Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1, so when user_count is an exact multiple of limit we report an extra phantom page with no members
next clamps offset with Math.min(offset + limit, user_count) so when you hit the last page offset becomes user_count and the backend returns zero members and a blank page
members now always runs with limit=50/offset even for non-automatic groups but the public group/members template has no pagination controls, so the UI never loads past the first page

coderabbit.text: totalPages calculation has off-by-one error when user_count is a multiple of limit - Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 yields an extra page for exact multiples, should use Math.ceil instead
next() function allows navigating to an empty page by clamping offset to user_count instead of the last valid offset
Usernames are not normalized before lookup in add_members - whitespace or blank entries will silently skip valid users
Pagination params (limit and offset) are not clamped - negative or very large values can trigger heavy queries or odd behavior
Test for automatic-group remove_member uses PUT instead of DELETE, which won't validate verb correctness
Guard condition in group/members.hbs checks model but loop iterates over members - if model is truthy but members is undefined/empty, table header renders with no rows
Stray closing tag with no matching opening tag in group/members.hbs template
Route parameter mismatch - routes provide :id but controller expects :group_id, causing 'param is missing' error
Hardcoded colors (#333 and #aaa) in SCSS instead of SCSS variables may break theme consistency
usernames input is not cleared after add operation, making it easy to accidentally re-add the same usernames

greptile.text: HTTP method should be DELETE, not PUT, to match the route and controller action

qodo-v2.text: Admin group pagination computes totalPages with Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1, which adds an extra page when user_count is an exact multiple of limit, allowing navigation to an offset equal to user_count and yielding an empty members list

The remove_member endpoint removes memberships via group.users.delete(user_id) but does not trigger the existing primary_group_id cleanup used by bulk username updates, leaving User.primary_group_id pointing at a group the user is no longer a member of and bypassing GroupUser counter-cache/callback semantics

devin.text: totalPages calculation is off-by-one when user_count is evenly divisible by limit - using Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 instead of Math.ceil(user_count / limit) causes an extra phantom page to be calculated, keeping the 'next' button enabled on the real last page and allowing navigation to an empty final page

Off-by-one error in totalPages calculation: Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 overestimates page count when user_count is divisible by limit, allowing navigation to empty results page
alias_level is silently dropped during group creation: the create action only sets name and visible, ignoring the alias_level parameter that the frontend sends
Spec uses wrong HTTP method for remove_member: test calls xhr :put but the route is DELETE, misrepresenting how the endpoint is actually reached

mra-max.text: Tests updated to cover new group membership endpoints and behavior including admin controller specs for index, update, refresh_automatic_groups, add_members, and remove_member flows
Admin-group.js.es6 controller includes client-side pagination helpers and separate usernames input, creating risk of desynchronization between UI state and server data

Admin groups controller gained create, update, add_members, remove_member, and refresh_automatic_groups actions with new routes - mismatch between new routes and controller actions risks 404/422 errors and regressions

GroupsController#members now returns JSON structure with members and pagination metadata instead of plain serialized array - gaps between implementation and test expectations could mask integration regressions
Client.en translation changes include updating selector_placeholder and adding group management labels - need to ensure UI messages align with the new UI

mra-ultra.text: Pagination totalPages computation uses floor(user_count / limit) + 1 which overcounts when user_count is an exact multiple of limit; should use Math.ceil(user_count / limit)
Removed afterModel hook and setupController calling model.findMembers() without awaiting leaves controller's 'members' property unset when template renders, breaking group.hbs template rendering
findMembers returns undefined instead of a promise when group's name is empty, causing silent failures or broken async flows for callers expecting a promise

Admin GroupsController heavily rewritten with altered behavior and endpoints (create, update, destroy, refresh_automatic_groups, add_members, remove_member) representing major API shift affecting authorization, data integrity, and client compatibility
Public Groups controller posts action returns paginated payload with meta section instead of plain list, breaking API change for clients relying on old response shape

mra-b.text: totalPages calculation is off-by-one when user_count is an exact multiple of limit - using Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1 instead of Math.ceil(user_count/limit) results in one extra page being displayed
findMembers returns undefined instead of a Promise when group name is empty, breaking callers that chain .then() on the result

Admin group membership modification API breaking change - membership changes moved from PUT /admin/groups/:id to new endpoints (PUT /admin/groups/:group_id/members for adds, DELETE /admin/groups/:group_id/members for removes) with different parameter shapes
Public groups#members endpoint response shape changed from array to object with members and meta keys, breaking API consumers expecting an array
HTTP status for attempting to modify automatic groups changed from 403 Forbidden to 422 Unprocessable Entity, affecting clients that branch on HTTP status codes

mra-a.text: totalPages off-by-one when user_count is divisible by limit - Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1 yields incorrect page count (e.g., 2 instead of 1 for 50/50), should use Math.ceil(user_count/limit)
findMembers returns undefined instead of a Promise for empty group name, causing TypeErrors at runtime since callers expect a thenable (regression from prior Ember.RSVP.resolve([]) return)
Group.create no longer accepts initial usernames - createWithUsernames behavior was removed, breaking atomic group creation with initial members in a single request

Template iteration changed from model to members but members may not exist in template scope since code sets model and calls model.findMembers() without explicitly setting controller.members, causing empty list rendering

Admin API create endpoint parameter shape changed from nested params (group[name]) to top-level params (params[:name]), breaking external callers and third-party integrations expecting the previous format

entelligence.text: Using Math.floor(...)+1 overcounts when user_count is a multiple of limit (e.g., 10/5 => 3 pages), making showingLast false on the real last page and allowing navigation to an empty page. Should use Math.ceil instead.
Dropping saveWithUsernames/createWithUsernames means the usernames entered in the UI are no longer sent during save/create, so members won't be added when saving a group.

Removing afterModel and the members assignment means the controller no longer receives the loaded members. model.findMembers() returns a promise but its result is now ignored, so any template/controller expecting members will see undefined.

Returning undefined when the group name is empty breaks the previous contract of always returning a promise; callers that chain .then() will now crash. Should return a resolved promise (e.g., empty array) to preserve the async API.

Both create and update now read attributes from the top-level params, but the previous update_put expected params[:group]. If clients still send { group: { ... } }, these actions will silently ignore input (e.g., name/visible won't update).

This change paginates all groups with a default limit of 50, whereas previously non-automatic groups returned the full member list. That's a breaking behavioral regression for clients that expect complete results without paging.

sourcery.text: Member routes are using `group_id` param, but routes will supply `:id`, which will raise on `params.require`. Using `params.require(:group_id)` in `add_members` will raise `ActionController::ParameterMissing` for valid requests. Use `params.require(:id)` instead to match the routes.

`remove_member` has the same `group_id` param issue as `add_members`. `remove_member` should load the group from `params[:id]` rather than `params[:group_id]`; otherwise DELETE `/admin/groups/:id/members` requests will raise a missing parameter error.

Pagination `totalPages` calculation is off by one when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit`. For example, `user_count = 100` and `limit = 50` yields `Math.floor(100 / 50) + 1 = 3`, but there are only 2 valid pages. Use `Math.ceil(this.get("user_count") / this.get("limit"))` instead.

In the new `group-member` view/template, the template references `member` explicitly (`{{avatar member ...}}`), but when used as `itemView="group-member"` inside `{{each member in members}}` the item view's context is the member itself; should reference `this` (or `view.content`) instead of `member` to avoid an undefined binding.

The `Group#findMembers` method now returns `undefined` when `name` is empty instead of an immediately-resolved promise; if any callers still assume a promise (e.g. previously chained `.then`), this can break their flow. Consider always returning a promise for API consistency.

cubic-dev.text: Wrong interpolation syntax for a client-side locale file. This file uses `{{variable}}` (Handlebars-style) for interpolation, but `%{variable}` (Ruby i18n style) is used here. The variables will render literally in the UI instead of being substituted.
`totalPages` has an off-by-one bug; it should use ceiling division instead of `floor(...) + 1`.

`group.users.delete(user_id)` passes a raw integer and bypasses `Group#remove`, which runs destroy callbacks that reset `primary_group_id`. Use the model's `remove` method with a User object, consistent with how `add_members` uses `group.add(user)`.

Returning `undefined` instead of a resolved promise breaks the method's promise-based contract. The previous code returned `Ember.RSVP.resolve([])`, ensuring callers could safely chain `.then()`. Any future (or current) caller that chains on `findMembers()` will get a TypeError when the name is empty.

HTTP method mismatch: this test uses `:put` but the success-case test for the same `remove_member` action uses `:delete`. This test likely hits the wrong route and doesn't actually verify the automatic-group protection on the `remove_member` endpoint.

The `findMembers()` promise is discarded (fire-and-forget), introducing a race condition. The old `afterModel` hook returned the promise so Ember waited for members to load before rendering. Now the template renders immediately with no members, and if the user navigates away before the AJAX completes, the `.then` callback will still set properties on the (now stale) model.
The remove control uses `<a>` without `href`, making it inaccessible via keyboard (not focusable). Use a `<button>` element instead, and add an `aria-label` so screen readers can identify the action.

The `self.findMembers()` return value is not returned from the `.then()` callback, so the outer promise resolves before the member list reload completes. Any caller chaining on `removeMember()` won't know when members are actually reloaded.
Negative `offset` (or `limit`) values will cause a PostgreSQL error and a 500 response. Clamp both to a minimum of 0.
No upper bound on `limit` — a client can pass `?limit=999999` to bypass pagination entirely. Cap it to a reasonable maximum (e.g., 1000).
Hardcoded hex colors `#333` and `#aaa` break theming. The rest of this file consistently uses SCSS variables like `$primary` for colors.
The `for="alias"` attribute on this label won't associate with the combo-box below because the component sets `name="alias"` but not `id="alias"`. Add `id="alias"` to the combo-box.

The `for="name"` attribute on this label won't associate with the text-field below because the component sets `name="name"` (HTML name attribute) but not `id="name"`. Add `id="name"` to the text-field to make the label clickable.

Adding a form without a submit handler means pressing Enter in the name field will submit the browser form and reload the page. Attach a submit action (or prevent default) so the form doesn't navigate away.

The `findMembers()` method caps `offset` at `user_count` (`Math.min(this.get("user_count"), ...)`), which means if the offset equals the total user count, the request will return zero results — an empty last page. The cap should account for the limit.
Clamp the offset to the last valid page start (user_count - limit) so offsets equal to the total count don't request an empty page.
Use strict equality (`===`) instead of loose equality (`==`) to avoid subtle type-coercion issues.
Stray semicolon after `}` creates an empty statement. The `;` should be either inside the braces (after `return`) or removed entirely.
Inconsistent quote style: `"model"` uses double quotes while adjacent strings (`'group'`, `'members'`) use single quotes.

mesa.text: The totalPages formula incorrectly calculates an extra page when user_count is an exact multiple of limit (e.g., 100 users with limit 50 returns 3 pages instead of 2). Should use Math.ceil(this.get("user_count") / this.get("limit")) instead

The offset clamping allows offset === user_count, which will fetch an empty page. After member additions/deletions, this can cause pagination desynchronization. Should clamp to Math.max(this.get("user_count") - this.get("limit"), 0) as the upper bound
Function returns undefined instead of a promise when the name is empty, which is a contract change from the previous Ember.RSVP.resolve([]). Callers expecting promise chaining may break
The username parsing doesn't normalize or trim whitespace. Should use usernames.split(",").map(&:strip).reject(&:blank?).uniq to handle input variations consistently
The limit parameter is not clamped to a safe maximum, allowing potential abuse or performance degradation from excessively large limit values. Should clamp to a maximum like 200

The response format changed from a simple serialized array to an object with members and meta properties, which is a breaking API change that could impact existing consumers (plugins, mobile apps, or other clients)

codeant.text: findMembers returns undefined when name is empty instead of returning Ember.RSVP.resolve([]), which breaks promise chains expecting a promise
Username splitting in add_members doesn't strip whitespace, so usernames like ' alice' won't match - should use usernames.split(',').map(&:strip)
BasicGroupSerializer output may not contain the basic_group wrapper expected by JS create() which expects resp.basic_group.id
Inconsistent HTTP status codes for automatic groups - destroy returns 422 but semantically 403 (Forbidden) may be more appropriate than 422 (Unprocessable Entity)
group.save is called after group.add/group.users.delete but group.add may already persist membership, making the extra save potentially unnecessary
find_group uses Group.find_by which may return nil, and guardian.ensure_can_see! may not handle nil properly - should add explicit not_found handling for 404
Indentation of protected method can_not_modify_automatic uses 4 spaces inside protected block - minor style inconsistency
Missing tests for add_members/remove_member with usernames containing spaces (edge case)

kodus.text: Use strong parameters in the create action to prevent mass assignment vulnerabilities - raw params are being used directly instead of require and permit
Inconsistent HTTP verb usage for remove_member action - route uses DELETE but test incorrectly uses PUT
Missing 'Only Admins' (value: 1) option in frontend aliasLevelOptions that exists in backend Group model ALIAS_LEVELS
Off-by-one error in pagination totalPages calculation - using Math.floor + 1 instead of Math.ceil causes extra empty page when user_count is exact multiple of limit
Unused return value from model.findMembers() in setupController - promise result is discarded so members list is never populated on controller
Race condition in removeMember and addMembers - both trigger findMembers() refresh which can cause inconsistent UI state when actions are triggered in quick succession

Group visibility incorrectly set to false on any update that doesn't explicitly include visible parameter - should only update when parameter is present

Routes defined inside resources block without collection/member wrapper cause params[:group_id] to be missing - controller expects group_id but route provides id
Wrong HTTP verb in automatic-group guard test for remove_member - test uses PUT but action is DELETE endpoint

claude-code.text: remove_member calls group.users.delete(user_id) which performs a direct SQL DELETE bypassing ActiveRecord callbacks including counter_cache, causing group.user_count to become stale after member removal. Should use group.remove(User.find(user_id)) instead which properly triggers counter cache updates.

totalPages is computed as Math.floor(user_count / limit) + 1 which produces an off-by-one error when user_count is an exact multiple of limit (e.g., 100/50 gives 3 instead of 2), allowing users to navigate to an empty page. Should use Math.ceil(user_count / limit) instead.

kodus-v2.text: Undefined iteration target in Handlebars template: the #each block iterates over 'members' instead of 'model', but 'members' is undefined in the template context since 'model' is the Ember route-provided property, causing zero rows to render
Mass assignment vulnerability in create action: params[:name] and params[:visible] are read directly without using require and permit, bypassing strong parameters whitelisting

Update action reads directly from raw params hash instead of using strong parameters with require and permit
Unvalidated user-controlled limit parameter allows bypassing the default of 50 by passing large values like 1000000

qodo-v2-2.text: The public group members template iterates 'members', but the group-members route only sets 'model' and never sets a 'members' property on the controller, so the template has no bound list to render and can show an empty table even though findMembers() populates model.members

Discourse.Group.findMembers() fetches a paginated member list with a default limit of 50, but the public group members page has no pagination controls or load-more behavior, so groups with more than 50 members cannot be fully browsed from the public UI

Admin group pagination computes totalPages using Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1, which overcounts when user_count is exactly divisible by limit, enabling navigation to a non-existent extra page and requests with offset == user_count that return empty member lists

cubic-v2.text: `findMembers()` is called without awaiting its result, introducing a race condition. The template may render before members are available. Either return the promise from `setupController` or move the async call back to `afterModel`/`beforeModel` where the router will wait for it.

Item-view template references `member`, but that alias is not available inside a separate `itemView` template. The row will render with missing member data, and clicking remove can throw when the controller calls `member.get(...)`.

`totalPages` is off by one when `user_count` is an exact multiple of `limit`, so the pager shows an extra page. Should use `Math.ceil(this.get("user_count") / this.get("limit"))` instead of `Math.floor(...) + 1`.

qodo-extended-summary.text: The public group members template iterates 'members', but the route only sets 'controller.model' to the group and never exposes a 'members' property, so the table can render empty even after findMembers() populates model.members

Admin group pagination calculates totalPages with Math.floor(user_count/limit)+1, which overstates pages when user_count is an exact multiple of limit and allows navigating to a non-existent final page
The 'next' action permits offset to become exactly user_count, producing an empty result set
Group creation from the client sends alias_level, but Admin::GroupsController#create never assigns it, so newly created groups cannot persist the selected alias level

group.users.delete(user_id) performs a raw SQL DELETE on the group_users join table, bypassing ActiveRecord callbacks, so the counter_cache 'user_count' on GroupUser is never decremented when a member is removed
The add_members action directly calls group.add(user) without checking if user is already a member, causing a database unique constraint violation when adding duplicate members

gemini.text: Race condition when updating match_count - concurrent requests could read the same value, increment in memory, and overwrite each other's changes, leading to incorrect counts. Should use atomic update operation instead.
Missing newline at end of file - Ruby convention and style guide recommends source files end with a newline

claude.text: Race condition in BlockedEmail.should_block? where multiple concurrent requests can cause lost updates to match_count due to read-modify-write pattern
Silent failure on save in BlockedEmail.should_block? - uses .save instead of .save! which silently swallows validation errors
BlockedEmail.should_block? modifies database records during validation which should be side-effect-free - increments counters even when validation fails for other reasons
Statistics tracking in validation causes stat pollution - failed user creations still increment stats making statistics inaccurate
Transaction issues - if user creation fails after validation the stats are still saved
Missing newline at end of file in lib/validators/email_validator.rb
Inconsistent use of 'and' keyword instead of '&&' operator in lib/validators/email_validator.rb line 13

qodo.text: Information exposure: users_controller#create returns errors and values including email in JSON response on failure - need to confirm endpoint is safe to expose detailed validation messages and that no sensitive attributes can be added to the sliced set inadvertently
Race condition: Block statistics updates in should_block? method use read-modify-write pattern and can lose increments under concurrent signups - should use atomic update or locking
Data consistency: Email uniqueness validation may allow duplicates differing only by case unless normalization or case-insensitive uniqueness is enforced with DB index backing

Client-side caching of rejected emails may miss equivalent emails with different casing/whitespace and can grow unbounded during repeated attempts - should normalize email before storing/checking and limit/clear the cache

copilot.text: Race condition in read-modify-write operation (lines 14-16) when multiple requests check the same email simultaneously, resulting in inaccurate match_count values due to non-atomic increment
Calling save without error handling means failures will be silently ignored, leading to inconsistent data

bugbot.text: When an email is added to rejectedEmails after server-side validation failure, the client-side validation displays a generic 'Please enter a valid email address' message instead of the actual rejection reason (blocked, already taken, or disallowed domain), causing user confusion

BlockedEmail.should_block? performs a case-sensitive database query, so blocking 'spam@example.com' won't block 'spam@EXAMPLE.COM' or 'SPAM@example.com' - emails should be normalized using Email.downcase for proper case-insensitive comparison

augment.text: email_in_restriction_setting? calls value =~ regexp which will raise if value is nil (e.g., missing email param) even though presence validation would already fail - need to guard validate_each / email_in_restriction_setting? against blank/nil values

BlockedEmail.should_block? mutates state (match_count/last_match_at) and is called from email validation which can run multiple times per request (e.g., user.valid? then user.save), inflating stats and causing extra writes for a single signup attempt

pushObject(result.values.email) will add null/undefined if the server response omits values.email or returns it blank, leaving a non-string entry in rejectedEmails - should ensure value is present before caching

The computed property depends on rejectedEmails.@each but @each is mainly for observing per-item properties and can be unreliable for array membership changes with primitive items like strings - may not recompute when pushObject is called depending on Ember version
Missing regression test covering nil/blank value when whitelist/blacklist settings are present to ensure validation doesn't raise unexpectedly

propel.text: Race condition in blocked email counter: Multiple concurrent signups hitting the same blocked email will each read the current match_count, add 1, and save. Because this is a read-modify-write sequence without any locking, later saves can clobber earlier increments and the statistics (including last_match_at) become inaccurate. Should use atomic database update instead.

emailValidation computed property depends on 'rejectedEmails.@each', but @each only reacts to property changes on array items, not to items being added/removed. After the server rejects an email and pushes it into rejectedEmails, the computed property doesn't recompute, so the UI still shows the email as valid. Should use 'rejectedEmails.[]' instead to track collection changes.

baz.text: BlockedEmail.should_block? does case-sensitive lookup using raw email while User.email preserves original casing, so blocking 'foo@example.com' won't catch 'Foo@example.com' - need to normalize/downcase both sides when storing/looking up blocked emails

EmailValidator calls BlockedEmail.should_block? with raw email but BlockedEmail.where is exact match and User never downcases/strips the address before validation, so uppercase or whitespace variants skip the block list - need to normalize (downcase+strip) before querying BlockedEmail or make lookup case-insensitive

coderabbit.text: Case-sensitive email lookup in BlockedEmail.should_block? creates bypass risk - User@Example.com can bypass a block on user@example.com
Email.downcase() only lowercases the domain part, not the local part, so it won't fix the case-sensitivity issue - need to use full downcase instead
BlockedEmail model needs a normalize_email callback to downcase emails before validation
BlockedEmail.should_block? should use email.downcase when querying to ensure case-insensitive matching

kg.text: Case-sensitive email matching in BlockedEmail.where(email: email).first allows users to bypass blocks by using different cases (e.g., 'Bad@spam.com' vs 'bad@spam.com'). Should use where('lower(email) = ?', email.downcase) and store emails in lowercase.

qodo-v2.text: BlockedEmail lookup uses exact string match, so case/whitespace variants of a blocked address can bypass blocking and allow duplicate blocked entries differing only by case - should canonicalize via Email.downcase(...).strip like other email lookups

Email validation calls BlockedEmail.should_block? which increments match_count and saves, causing DB writes during validation - UsersController#create runs validations at least twice (valid? then save), so a single blocked signup attempt will double-increment stats and add unnecessary DB write load
EmailValidator always queries BlockedEmail which will raise an exception if the new blocked_emails table hasn't been migrated yet during deploy - should use table_exists? guard pattern

Non-atomic increment of match_count causes lost updates under concurrency - the read-modify-write pattern in should_block? allows two concurrent processes to read the same value, both increment locally, and both write the same result, losing one count

greptile-v4.text: Non-atomic match_count increment causes race condition - read-modify-write operation without database-level locking will cause lost updates under concurrent load when two simultaneous requests read the same match_count, increment in memory, and save

Shared mutable array across all controller instances - rejectedEmails array defined directly in extend() is placed on class prototype and shared across all instances, causing rejected emails from one session to persist and pollute subsequent sessions

Frontend churn risk in CreateAccountController.js - Addition of rejectedEmails state and related logic introduces multiple changes (new state property, runtime check, computed bindings, server error handling) across multiple locations in the file, raising risk of regressions and inconsistent state management

Information leakage in user creation error payload - The create action renders JSON error response including errors: user.errors.to_hash and values: user.attributes.slice containing sensitive user input including email, enabling potential user enumeration attacks

result.values.email can be undefined/null but is not guarded before pushing into rejectedEmails, causing undefined/null to be added and incorrectly blocking future submissions with falsy email values

BlockedEmail.should_block? increments match_count and updates last_match_at inside EmailValidator#validate_each, causing stats to be incremented multiple times per signup attempt when valid? is called more than once

entelligence.text: The rejection check for blocked emails is case-sensitive, allowing users to bypass the block by changing email casing (e.g., 'User@a.com' vs 'user@a.com'). The comparison should be case-insensitive by normalizing emails before comparing or storing them normalized.

sourcery.text: Use the array change key `rejectedEmails.[]` instead of `@each` for a string array dependency - `@each` won't reliably track changes for primitive strings, only for observing properties on objects

kodus.text: State leak from mutable property on controller prototype - initializing a mutable array (Em.A([])) on a prototype means all instances share the same array, causing rejected emails from one attempt to affect all subsequent attempts since Ember controllers are singletons
Race condition in counter increment - the read-modify-write operation on match_count is not atomic, causing lost increments under concurrent load leading to inaccurate statistics

Case-sensitive email uniqueness validation allows duplicate accounts - uniqueness: true is case-sensitive by default, permitting registration of emails differing only by case which contradicts the case-insensitive email_hash method
Inconsistent nullability for timestamp columns - t.timestamps creates nullable columns on Rails versions prior to 5.0, inconsistent with other columns explicitly defined with null: false
Regular expression for domain validation allows partial matches - blacklisting 'mail.com' incorrectly blocks 'gmail.com' because the regex is not anchored to the end of the string

Shared mutable array across all controller instances - Em.A([]) is evaluated once at class-definition time producing a single array object shared by every instance, causing previously-blocked emails to appear invalid immediately when modal is reopened
Security bypass in whitelist/blacklist check - regex without end-of-string anchor allows attacker to register with 'user@vaynermedia.com.evil.com' when whitelist is set to 'vaynermedia.com'
