No updated specs for the changes to UsersController#create
No updated specs for the modified User model validations
email_validator.rb file is missing a trailing newline

kodus-v2.text: Whitelist bypass via unanchored regex - the regex `@(#{domains})` matches the whitelisted domain as a substring, so `user@vaynermedia.com.evil.com` passes a whitelist of `vaynermedia.com` because `@vaynermedia.com` appears as a substring. An attacker can register any domain like `vaynermedia.com.attacker.com` to bypass the whitelist. The regex should be anchored to the end of the string with `\z`

qodo-v2-2.text: BlockedEmail.should_block? performs an exact string match on the submitted email without normalizing (downcase/strip) the email before lookup, allowing bypass of blocked entries by changing casing or whitespace
Duplicate blocked_email rows can be created that differ only by case due to lack of normalization

qodo-v22.text: CreateAccountController.emailValidation observes rejectedEmails.@each instead of .[] for array membership changes, so pushing a rejected email may not reliably invalidate the field and can allow repeated submissions for a server-rejected email

BlockedEmail.should_block? updates match_count/last_match_at as a side effect of validation, but UsersController#create calls user.valid? and then user.save, causing blocked-email stats to be incremented twice for a single signup attempt

BlockedEmail.should_block? increments match_count with a read-modify-write and save, so concurrent requests matching the same blocked email can lose increments and corrupt match_count/last_match_at statistics
BlockedEmail.should_block? queries by the raw email string and BlockedEmail uniqueness is case-sensitive, so a blocked email can be bypassed by varying email casing

qodo-extended.text: BlockedEmail.should_block? matches emails with exact string equality, so users can bypass blocking by changing email casing (e.g., BLOCK@x vs block@x). The client-side rejectedEmails cache is also case-sensitive, so the UI will not consistently prevent resubmission with different casing.

EmailValidator emits I18n.t('user.email.blocked') but only server.en.yml defines this key, so non-English server locales will show 'translation missing' strings. The client also has no user.email.blocked translation, so the UI cannot present a blocked-specific inline reason.

BlockedEmail.should_block? updates match_count via read-modify-write and calls record.save without checking success, so concurrent attempts can lose increments and failures are silently ignored. This makes match_count/last_match_at unreliable and adds unnecessary write load on every match.

BlockedEmail.should_block? writes to the database (incrementing match_count) during model validation. In users_controller.rb#create, user.valid? and user.save both trigger the full validation chain, so should_block? is called twice per blocked registration attempt, double-counting every match.

In EmailValidator#email_in_restriction_setting?, the regex @(#{domains}) lacks an end-of-string anchor. For whitelist mode, user@company.com.evil.org falsely matches company.com and passes validation; for blacklist mode, user@mailinator.com.legit.org is incorrectly blocked.

The frontend indiscriminately adds any email that receives backend errors to rejectedEmails. If an email fails for non-block reasons (e.g., 'already taken'), it will be permanently rejected on the client side with a generic 'invalid' message.

cubic-v2.text: Normalize the email before querying in BlockedEmail.should_block?; otherwise blocked addresses can be bypassed by changing case
EmailValidator no longer rejects malformed email addresses, so validates :email, email: true can now accept values like sam@sam or sam

should_block? is a predicate method but has write side effects (increments match_count, sets last_match_at, saves record), violating command-query separation; callers expect a ? method to be a pure read but every invocation silently mutates the database

qodo-extended-summary.text: CreateAccountController caches any server-side email error into rejectedEmails and always reports it as user.email.invalid, so emails rejected for other reasons (e.g., already-taken, domain-not-allowed, blocked) become incorrectly treated as syntactically invalid in the modal

BlockedEmail.should_block? does a case-sensitive exact match on the raw email string, so a blocked entry can be bypassed by changing casing/normalization because User emails are not downcased before validation

BlockedEmail.should_block? performs a database write (record.match_count += 1; record.save) inside the EmailValidator#validate_each callback, violating Rails conventions that validations should be side-effect-free and causing double-counting since user.valid? and user.save both trigger validations in the controller

gemini.text: Float properties might conflict with the newly applied flexbox layouts, causing inconsistent and unpredictable behavior

claude.text: The `@mixin order($val)` incorrectly maps the `order` value to `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` without accounting for the different starting values between old and new flexbox specifications. The old 2009 flexbox spec uses 1-based indexing while modern flexbox uses 0-based indexing. The mixin should use `$val + 1` for `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group` to ensure correct ordering in older browsers.

qodo.text: Flexbox mixins use legacy vendor syntaxes with non-equivalent properties/values across implementations (e.g., -webkit-box-align and -webkit-box-ordinal-group), which may not produce intended layout across target browsers, especially for align-items(baseline) and order()

.contents is now a flex container and .panel uses margin-left: auto plus order(3), which may break header child element ordering/spacing in different header states (docked/undocked) and could break existing float-based positioning assumptions

Switching .small-action to flex and changing .small-action-desc padding and paragraph margins can alter vertical rhythm and wrapping, potentially breaking avatar/description alignment and multi-line behavior on narrow viewports and for long custom messages

bugbot.text: The button element inside .small-action has float: right which is ignored after the parent becomes a flex container via @include flexbox(), causing the button to lose its right alignment. Should use margin-left: auto instead of float: right for proper flexbox alignment.

copilot.text: Comment should have a space after `//` for consistency with other comments in the file
Trailing whitespace should be removed from the end of the line
Missing space after colon in SCSS property declaration for consistency with formatting conventions

augment.text: For legacy -webkit-box/-moz-box flexbox syntax, *-box-ordinal-group is 1-based (whereas modern order is 0-based), so using $val directly can shift ordering by 1 in older browsers - need to offset the *-box-ordinal-group value
-ms-align-items is not a recognized MS flexbox property (IE10/11 use -ms-flex-align instead), keeping it emits invalid CSS declarations and makes debugging harder

propel.text: Legacy flexbox implementations (-webkit-box / -moz-box) treat *-box-ordinal-group as one-based while modern order property is zero-based, causing elements to render one spot earlier in old WebKit/Gecko builds when the same value is used for both

coderabbit.text: Legacy flexbox order off-by-one error: -webkit-box-ordinal-group and -moz-box-ordinal-group use 1-based indexing while modern order uses 0-based indexing, causing mis-ordering in older WebKit and Firefox browsers when passing $val directly to legacy properties

greptile.text: -webkit-box-ordinal-group and -moz-box-ordinal-group require values starting from 1, not 0. If $val is 0, this will cause incorrect rendering in older browsers
Missing space after colon on line 122 in align-items mixin

kg.text: Invalid CSS property `-ms-align-items` - this property does not exist. IE10 uses `-ms-flex-align` instead, which is already included above. This invalid property should be removed to avoid potential CSS parsing issues.

qodo-v2.text: The server-rendered header (_header.html.erb) places .panel inside a Bootstrap .row, but the PR removes float: right and replaces it with flex-only positioning (margin-left: auto + order). Because .row is not a flex container, the login panel will no longer right-align and may drop below the title for logged-out pages.

The new flexbox()/inline-flex() mixins emit display: -moz-box / display: -moz-inline-box, but align-items() does not emit -moz-box-align. This creates inconsistent cross-browser behavior: any case that falls back to the -moz box model won't get the intended alignment (e.g., badge bullet baseline alignment).

devin.text: The order mixin passes $val directly to -webkit-box-ordinal-group, but this old flexbox property is 1-indexed while the standard order property is 0-indexed, causing inconsistent ordering behavior between old WebKit browsers and modern browsers. The correct mapping should be -webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val + 1 (and same for -moz-box-ordinal-group)

The @include order(2) on .extra-info-wrapper has no effect because .extra-info-wrapper is not a direct child of the flex container .contents - it is wrapped inside an Ember component div for header-extra-info, and the CSS order property only works on direct children of a flex container

greptile-v4.text: The align-items mixin includes `-ms-align-items: $alignment` which is not a valid CSS property and was never part of any Microsoft/IE spec - this is dead code that should be removed

The `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group` properties in the order mixin use a 1-based ordinal group system, while the modern `order` property uses a 0-based integer - passing the same `$val` to both causes an off-by-one visual order difference in old WebKit browsers, should pass `$val + 1` to the old-spec properties

The `button { float: right }` inside `.small-action` is ignored because floats have no effect on flex items after the parent was converted to a flex container - this causes the delete/remove button to lose its right-aligned position and is a visual regression

sourcery.text: The `-ms-align-items` declaration is not a valid legacy flexbox property and can be dropped - for IE10/11's legacy flexbox, only `-ms-flex-align` is valid; `-ms-align-items` is ignored

The `order` mixin misaligns values between legacy `box-ordinal-group` (1-based, default 1) and modern `order` (0-based, default 0) - using `$val` for both means `order: 0` produces `-webkit-box-ordinal-group: 0`, which changes layout vs the default; should use `$val + 1` for the legacy properties

In the `@mixin align-items($alignment)` mixin, the legacy properties (`-webkit-box-align`, `-ms-flex-align`) expect older value keywords (e.g., `start`/`end` instead of `flex-start`/`flex-end`), so passing modern values may not behave as intended in older engines; consider normalizing or mapping the values before applying the prefixed properties

cubic-dev.text: `-webkit-box-align` and `-ms-flex-align` do not accept the same values as `align-items`. Values like `flex-start`/`flex-end` need to be mapped to `start`/`end` for these legacy prefixed properties. Without this mapping, the prefixes are ineffective for the very browsers they target.

`-webkit-box-ordinal-group` is 1-based, while the standard `order` property is 0-based. Passing the same `$val` to both means old-WebKit ordering will be off by one. Typically this is handled with `-webkit-box-ordinal-group: $val + 1`.
`-ms-align-items` is not a valid CSS property. The correct IE10 prefixed property is `-ms-flex-align`, which is already declared on the previous line. This line has no effect and should be removed.

This replaces standard CSS with vendor-prefix mixins, moving in the opposite direction of modern best practices. The native `display: inline-flex` and `align-items: baseline` properties are universally supported without prefixes. The mixins generate ~10 extra lines of unnecessary vendor-prefixed CSS output, adding bloat rather than optimizing performance as the PR title claims.

Adding `flexbox()` to `.small-action` silently disables `float: left` / `float: right` on its children (`.topic-avatar`, `.avatar`, `button`). Per the CSS spec, `float` has no effect on flex items. If the floats were intentional for positioning, the layout will shift; if they're now redundant, they should be removed to avoid confusion.
Inconsistent formatting: this mixin uses 4-space indentation (rest of file uses 2-space), and `align-items:$alignment` is missing the space after the colon.

mesa.text: The `order` property maps incorrectly to old flexbox syntax - `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group` require the value to be incremented by 1 (`$val + 1`) because the old spec used 1-based indexing while modern flexbox uses 0-based

The `order` mixin has a critical semantic bug: `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group` use 1-based indexing while modern `order` property uses 0-based indexing, causing different visual ordering in browsers using the legacy box model

The `align-items` mixin incorrectly passes modern flexbox values (like `flex-start`, `flex-end`) directly to `-webkit-box-align`, which expects legacy syntax values (`start`, `end`, `center`, `baseline`, `stretch`), causing silent failures in older webkit implementations

The padding change from `0.5em 0 0.5em 4em` to `0 1.5%` represents a substantial visual change beyond just structural flexbox conversion, removing 4em left padding and 0.5em vertical padding which will significantly alter the layout

Converting `.contents` from float-based layout to flexbox and using `margin-left: auto` with `order: 3` on `.panel` changes visual ordering semantics which may affect keyboard navigation and screen reader experience
The `order` mixin bugs (1-based vs 0-based indexing issue) could cause inconsistent layouts across browsers when using order property
Critical syntax error in header.scss - missing closing brace for `.contents` block and removed `.title` selector will break header layout and cascade incorrect nesting to subsequent selectors
Incomplete migration strategy creating a fragile hybrid state - some components adopt flexbox while others retain float-based layouts, risking unexpected layout interactions
Unnecessary vendor prefixes for browsers out of support (IE10, Safari 6-8) adds bloat without benefit - should rely on Autoprefixer based on declared browser support targets
Semantic misuse of flexbox `order` property applied to elements without confirmed flex container parents, rendering the property ineffective

Incomplete and fragmented migration pattern mixes float and flexbox within the same components (e.g., `.extra-info-wrapper` uses flex `order` while children still float), creating architectural brittleness
Manual vendor prefix strategy perpetuates obsolete 2009/2011 flexbox syntax instead of relying on modern unprefixed flexbox with Autoprefixer
Structural layout changes bundled with significant spacing and padding modifications make it impossible to isolate visual regression risk

codeant.text: order() mixin incorrectly maps flex order values to box-ordinal-group - box-ordinal-group is 1-based while flex order is 0-based, so $val should be $val + 1 for box-ordinal-group prefixes
Unnecessary/duplicated vendor prefixes in mixins when autoprefixer may already handle this, increasing maintenance burden and risk of mismatch
.title block with float:left was removed from header.scss but no replacement styling was added - verify .title still lays out correctly in the header
order(2) added to .extra-info-wrapper in topic.scss but order only affects flex items - need to verify parent container is display:flex
Mixed float + flex inside .small-action component - .topic-avatar and .avatar still use float:left inside a flex container, which is unnecessary and may cause layout quirks
align-items mixin uses -webkit-box-align and -ms-flex-align which may expect different value names for some alignments in older implementations
Padding and margin changes in topic-post.scss (p margins set to 0 etc.) may cause visual regressions - needs visual testing

claude-code.text: Mobile layout regression: The padding on `.small-action-desc` changed from `0.5em 0 0.5em 4em` to `0 1.5%`, but `mobile/topic-post.scss:521` still has `margin-left: -40px` on `.custom-message`. That negative margin was designed to offset the old ~64px left padding; with only ~4.8px of padding on a 320px mobile viewport, the custom-message now overflows ~35px past the left edge of its container

`.contents` in header.hbs still has the `clearfix` class, whose `::before`/`::after` pseudo-elements become unnecessary flex items when the container is `display: flex` — the `clear: both` on `::after` is a no-op in flex context

`@include order(2)` on `.extra-info-wrapper` (topic.scss:29) is dead CSS because `.extra-info-wrapper` is a grandchild of the flex container (Ember wraps `header-extra-info` in a `div.ember-view`), and `order` only applies to direct children
`-ms-align-items` on line 121 is not a valid CSS property (the correct `-ms-flex-align` is already on line 120, making this dead code)

The `order` mixin passes `$val` directly to `-webkit-box-ordinal-group` and `-moz-box-ordinal-group`, but these legacy properties use 1-based indexing while CSS `order` is 0-based — the mixin should pass `$val + 1` for correct behavior

qodo-v2-2.text: Clearfix breaks flex header - .d-header .contents is now a flex container, but the Ember header renders div with clearfix class which generates ::before/::after pseudo-elements with content that become extra flex items, creating unwanted leading/trailing gaps and preventing .panel from being flush-right

No-ember header loses right panel - The server-rendered header places .panel inside a .row that is not a flex container, but the PR removes the old float: right layout and replaces it with flex-only margin-left: auto/order, causing the login panel to no longer align right on no_ember and noscript render paths

qodo-v22.text: The .d-header .contents element is converted to a flex container but still has the .clearfix class applied, which injects ::before/::after pseudo-elements that become additional flex items under flex layout, causing unexpected spacing/ordering artifacts in the header

qodo-extended.text: Ember header uses clearfix class on .contents which is now a flex container, causing .clearfix::before/::after pseudo-elements (with display: table) to become flex items and potentially distort header alignment/spacing

header.scss removed .panel { float: right; } and replaced it with flex-only alignment (margin-left: auto), but the server-rendered header partial nests .title and .panel inside a Bootstrap .row (float grid), not a flex container, so the panel will no longer right-align in the server-rendered header

The order() mixin passes the same $val to both -webkit-box-ordinal-group (1-based indexing) and standard CSS order (0-based indexing), causing off-by-one error in old WebKit browsers (Safari < 6.1, Chrome < 21)

The align-items() mixin includes -ms-align-items: $alignment which is not a valid Microsoft vendor-prefixed property and will be silently ignored by all browsers, while the correct -ms-flex-align is already present in the same mixin

The .title { float: left; } rule was removed from header.scss and replaced with flexbox on .contents, but in the non-JS fallback header (_header.html.erb), .title is a child of .row (not a direct child of .contents), so it loses its float:left positioning with no replacement, breaking the fallback header layout

cubic-v2.text: Adding flexbox to `.small-action` while its children (`.topic-avatar`, `.avatar`, `button`) still use `float: left`/`float: right` is contradictory — floats are ignored on flex items. These float declarations should be removed or replaced with flex-based alignment (e.g., `margin-left: auto` on the button) to avoid unexpected layout results.
Keep paragraph spacing inside `.custom-message`; zeroing `p` margins collapses multi-paragraph cooked messages into one block.
This removes the only right-alignment rule for the ERB header's `.panel`; `margin-left: auto` only works when `.panel` is a flex item, but `_header.html.erb` nests it inside `.row`.

The `align-items` mixin emits `-ms-align-items`, which is not a valid vendor-prefixed CSS property. The correct IE prefix is `-ms-flex-align` (already present in the mixin). The invalid `-ms-align-items` line in the mixin should be removed to avoid confusion.

qodo-extended-summary.text: Header panel misaligned: .panel was converted from float-based alignment to flex alignment (margin-left: auto and order(3)), but .panel is not a direct child of the flex container (.contents), so the flex alignment won't work and the login panel won't align to the right edge

Non-standard -ms-align-items property in @mixin align-items is not a valid CSS property and will be silently ignored by all browsers - the correct IE10 vendor-prefixed equivalent is -ms-flex-align which is already present

Floats and clear inside flex container: .small-action now uses flexbox but its children .topic-avatar and button still have float: left and float: right respectively, and the container has clear: both - these float properties are ignored on flex items per CSS spec, indicating incomplete migration

-webkit-box-ordinal-group off-by-one: The @mixin order($val) passes $val directly to -webkit-box-ordinal-group, but the old -webkit-box spec uses 1-based ordinal groups while CSS3 order is 0-based, so the correct mapping should be $val + 1

gemini.text: The method can be made more efficient by parsing the website URI only once instead of calling URI(website.to_s) multiple times - storing the parsed URI in a local variable will improve performance

Missing test case for the scenario where the website and Discourse instance are different subdomains of the same parent domain (e.g., www.example.com and forum.example.com) to ensure the elsif block logic is robustly tested against regressions

qodo.text: Security concern: model.website is user-supplied and rendered into an href without validation, potentially enabling unsafe links with dangerous schemes like javascript:

The conditional attribute method is defined as include_website_name (without a ?) but many Rails/ActiveModel serializer patterns expect include_<attr>? for conditional inclusion, which may cause website_name to be included/excluded unexpectedly
website_name method repeatedly parses the URL (URI(website.to_s)) and relies on rescue nil for control flow, which is inefficient and could cause inconsistencies
website_name method needs validation for edge cases like uppercase hosts, trailing dots, ports, IDNs/punycode, and URLs without a path
Template renders user-controlled model.website into an href without verified upstream sanitization/validation to prevent dangerous schemes or malformed URLs

bugbot.text: The method `include_website_name` is missing the required `?` suffix. Active Model Serializers uses `include_#{attribute}?` convention to conditionally include attributes. Without the `?`, this method won't be called as a conditional, and `website_name` will always be serialized (potentially as `null` when no website is set) instead of being omitted when `website` is not present.

The `website_name` attribute bypasses trust level protection since it's not in the `untrusted_attributes` list, while the `website` attribute is protected. This means the user's website domain can be exposed even when `website` itself is intentionally hidden for privacy reasons.

The `website_name` method repeatedly performs the same expensive operations: `URI(website.to_s)` is parsed four times, `website_host.split('.')` is called three times, and `discourse_host.split('.')` is called three times. These values could be computed once and stored in local variables.

copilot.text: The `include_website_name` method is missing the trailing `?`, so ActiveModel::Serializer will not treat it as the inclusion hook for the `website_name` attribute, causing `website_name` to be serialized whenever it has a value regardless of inclusion conditions. Should be renamed to `include_website_name?`.

`website_name` is derived directly from `website` but is not treated as an untrusted attribute, so low-trust users whose `website` is intentionally hidden via `untrusted_attributes` / `restrict_user_fields?` will still leak their website through `website_name`. A TL0 user viewed anonymously can have their website exposed via this new field.
`website_name` should be subject to the same untrusted gating as `website` (by adding it to `untrusted_attributes` or checking `scope.restrict_user_fields?`).

The new `website_name` specs only cover the happy path for a regular user and do not verify that `website_name` remains hidden for TL0 users viewed anonymously. Should add a spec in the existing 'with a TL0 user seen as anonymous' context that asserts `json` does not include a `:website_name` key when `untrusted_attributes` are filtered.

augment.text: In website_name, the logic doesn't cover the case where the instance host is the apex domain (e.g. example.com) but the user website is a subdomain (e.g. www.example.com/user); in that case this branch returns only www.example.com without the path. Subdomains of the instance domain may need to include the path as well.

The conditional serializer helper is named include_website_name (missing ?) instead of include_website_name? like all other helpers, which means it won't gate serialization as expected and may cause website_name to be emitted unconditionally.

propel.text: The `include_website_name` guard does not respect `scope.restrict_user_fields?` like other untrusted attributes (e.g. `website`) do, causing `website_name` to be serialized for TL0 users viewed anonymously while `website` is suppressed, leaking the domain/path

Using `"." << website_host` mutates the string literal `"."` which Ruby reuses, causing the suffix to keep growing between requests and would raise `FrozenError` under `# frozen_string_literal: true`, making host comparisons incorrect

baz.text: When the instance hostname is the base domain and the user's website is on a subdomain like www.example.com, no branch matches and the path is not appended - same-site URLs with www or other subdomain suffix don't get the path appended as intended

include_website_name method lacks the required '?' suffix, so the serializer never gates the website_name attribute - when website is hidden via untrusted_attributes for anonymous/TL0 users, website_name still comes through and leaks the website host

coderabbit.text: include_website_name method is missing the trailing question mark (?) required by ActiveModelSerializers, so the predicate won't be invoked and website_name will serialize even when user fields are restricted (e.g., TL0/anonymous users viewing other profiles), leaking private data

greptile.text: Query parameters and URL fragments (like `?id=123#section`) are stripped from the path because only `URI(website.to_s).path` is used
`URI(website.to_s)` is parsed multiple times (lines 138, 143, 146, 149) instead of being parsed once and reused
Subdomain matching logic doesn't handle cases where domains have different numbers of segments (e.g., `example.co.uk` vs `forum.example.com`) or country-code TLDs correctly

qodo-v2.text: website_name is serialized without the TL0-anonymous 'untrusted attributes' restriction, so anonymous users may see a TL0 user's website text even when website is intentionally hidden
The template renders website link based on model.website_name while the href uses model.website, which can produce a broken/empty link if website is hidden but website_name is exposed
include_website_name method is missing the required '?' convention used elsewhere for conditional include hooks, so it won't properly act as a conditional include

devin.text: Missing `?` suffix on `include_website_name` method makes the inclusion guard a no-op - ActiveModel::Serializer requires `include_website_name?` to recognize it as an inclusion guard, but the method is named `include_website_name` without the `?`
website_name is serialized even when website is blank/nil, resulting in unnecessary nil values in the JSON payload
website_name leaks the user's website hostname to anonymous viewers of TL0 user profiles because it's not in untrusted_attributes and its inclusion guard is broken, violating privacy protection

greptile-v4.text: URI(website.to_s) is parsed up to three times within a single call to website_name - once for .host and again for .path. The rescue nil only guards the first parse; subsequent calls would raise unguarded. Should store the parsed URI in a local variable once.
String mutation with << operator: '.' << website_host uses the mutating append operator on a string literal. Using + is clearer and idiomatic Ruby for one-off concatenation.

include_website_name is missing the ? suffix that every other conditional-inclusion guard in the file uses. Depending on the ActiveModelSerializers version, the non-? form may not be recognised and the attribute could be serialised unconditionally even when website is blank.

Missing test for sibling-subdomain case (Case 2) - the website_name method has three distinct branches but the sibling-subdomain branch (e.g. www.example.com as the website host on a forum.example.com Discourse instance) has no corresponding test.

mra-max.text: Href uses user-provided URL without schema validation - user profile rendering binds href to model.website allowing javascript: URLs which could cause XSS when clicked
UserSerializer exposes website_name field with full URL path to TL0/anonymous users, potentially leaking internal routing structure and user data

Removal of client-side computed property websiteName without ensuring full parity in model/serializer could cause UI inconsistencies if website_name is not consistently available across all serializers or scopes
website_name is not included in untrusted_attributes macro and uses include_website_name method without trailing '?' which deviates from established pattern for gating sensitive fields

Access-control gating around website_name and untrusted attributes may be inconsistent - if gating logic is misaligned with internal policy, sensitive fields could be exposed or omitted inconsistently
URL/domain decision logic relies on Discourse.current_hostname which is a core environmental dependency that could affect website_name values across multisite setups
Tests for website_name behavior are tightly coupled to hostname stubs and exact string assertions which can lead to flaky tests in different environments or when URL parsing changes
No CHANGELOG entry documenting the new website_name exposure in the user serializer
PR description is minimal and does not summarize the change, rationale, or security/privacy implications
No documentation explaining the new website_name field or its privacy implications
Inline comments indicate property rename from websiteName to profileBackground but may not be in sync with runtime behavior

mra-nano.text: Frontend controller removes legacy websiteName property while backend serializer adds website_name, creating backward-compatibility risk for client code still referencing model.websiteName
XSS risk from user-provided URL used in href without URL scheme validation - user could set website to javascript: URL which would execute arbitrary script when clicked

mra-ultra.text: New website_name serializer method introduces complex hostname logic with edge cases (unusual TLDs, IPv6 addresses, URLs with ports) not covered by tests, posing regression and maintainability risks
Potential XSS/JS-link risk from user-supplied website URL in href - if attacker sets website to javascript: URL or other non-http/https schemes, clicking could execute arbitrary code
Link to user-supplied website URL lacks explicit relay protections (noopener) for external links, enabling potential phishing/tab-nabbing
URL schemes should be validated and whitelisted on input (allow only http/https) and sanitized or canonicalized when stored

mra-b.text: User-supplied website field is used directly as href attribute without sanitization, allowing potential XSS via javascript: URI or other unsafe protocols when a malicious user sets their website field

The include_website_name method is defined without a trailing question mark, but ActiveModelSerializers expects include_website_name? convention, causing the conditional inclusion logic to not work properly
Missing website_name in TL0 untrusted_attributes test, so there is no coverage verifying it is hidden for restricted users

mra-a.text: User-supplied URL used directly in href with target="_blank" without rel="noopener" or rel="noreferrer", enabling reverse tabnabbing attacks where the opened page can access window.opener and navigate the original site to a phishing page
User-supplied URL in href lacks scheme validation, allowing javascript: or data: URIs that can execute arbitrary script when clicked

New attribute :website_name is exposed in user serializer but not included in untrusted_attributes list, causing privacy leak where website information is visible to viewers who were previously prevented from seeing the :website field

Frontend/backend naming mismatch: template references model.website_name (snake_case) but Ember typically uses camelCase property names, potentially causing undefined or blank values if attribute transforms are in place

entelligence.text: Adding `website_name` to serialized attributes makes it visible to all viewers, but it is not covered by `untrusted_attributes` and there's no `include_website_name?` guard, which can leak a user's website host to anonymous/TL0 users even when `website` is hidden
Need to add `:website_name` to `untrusted_attributes` to properly restrict visibility
Need to implement `include_website_name?` method that respects `restrict_user_fields?` and checks `website.present?`

sourcery.text: Using `"." << website_host` mutates the receiver string, which can behave unpredictably with shared or frozen string literals (e.g., `frozen_string_literal: true` will raise)
In `website_name`, consider parsing the URI only once and reusing both the parsed object and the `host`/`path` to avoid repeated `URI(website.to_s)` calls

The `website_name` logic does multiple `split('.')` operations on `website_host` and `discourse_host`; factoring these into local variables would make the branching easier to follow and less error-prone
Rescuing all exceptions with `URI(website.to_s).host rescue nil` is too broad; it would be safer to rescue only `URI::InvalidURIError` or validate the URL earlier to avoid masking unexpected errors
The host comparison logic in `website_name` is intricate; adding small helper methods for 'same host', 'same parent domain', etc. would make the intent clearer and reduce edge-case bugs

cubic-dev.text: include_website_name method is missing the ? predicate suffix required by ActiveModelSerializers, causing the method to never be called by AMS, so website_name is always serialized regardless of whether website is present

mesa.text: `rescue nil` catches all exceptions broadly, which can hide unexpected runtime errors and make debugging harder. Should rescue `URI::InvalidURIError` explicitly or use a safe URL parser helper to handle malformed URLs intentionally while keeping the failure mode clear.

The subdomain comparison logic `website_host.split('.')[1..-1].join('.') == discourse_host.split('.')[1..-1].join('.')` only strips one subdomain level, which can misclassify deeper subdomain structures. For example, if website is `a.b.example.com` and instance is `forum.example.com`, this will incorrectly compare `b.example.com` vs `example.com`. Should use a more robust domain parsing approach (e.g., eTLD+1 aware library or shared utility) to reliably compare registrable domains.

codeant.text: include_website_name method name is wrong - should be include_website_name? with trailing question mark for serializer inclusion logic to work correctly
Using ends_with? instead of Ruby's standard end_with? method, which may be fragile if ActiveSupport alias is not available
URI parsing is done multiple times (up to 3 times) instead of parsing once and reusing components
Host comparison should use downcase for case-insensitive comparison since hosts are case-insensitive
Need to handle empty path properly - URI.path returns empty string for root URLs
Missing decision and handling for query string parameters in URLs (e.g., http://example.com/user?foo=1)
Missing handling for ports in URLs (e.g., example.com:3000) - URI.host excludes port
Missing test coverage for case insensitivity in hostname comparisons
Missing test coverage for URLs with query strings and fragments
Missing test coverage for URLs with ports
Missing test coverage for root path URLs (http://example.com/) to ensure no unexpected trailing slash
Missing test coverage for nil or invalid website values to ensure no exceptions are raised
Need to verify no other code references the removed websiteName computed property in controllers
Complex logic for detecting parent domain vs different subdomain needs clarifying comments

kodus.text: Incomplete domain matching logic drops the URL path when a user's website is a subdomain of the Discourse instance's domain. The logic correctly handles when the instance is a subdomain of the website, but not the reverse, leading to inconsistent and less useful link text on user profiles.

claude-code.text: The `website_name` attribute is not included in `untrusted_attributes`, so it remains visible to anonymous users viewing TL0 profiles even though `website` is correctly hidden, leaking website information that should be restricted

`include_website_name` method (line 153) is missing the trailing `?` - ActiveModel::Serializer requires the method to be named `include_website_name?` for conditional attribute inclusion, causing `website_name` to always be serialized instead of being conditionally omitted

kodus-v2.text: Missing `?` suffix on `include_website_name` method makes it invisible to ActiveModel::Serializer's conditional inclusion mechanism, causing `website_name` to always be serialized including for users with no website set and for TL0 users viewed anonymously, leaking data that should be hidden

Duplicate `URI(website.to_s)` parsing occurs up to three times within `website_name` method with inconsistent error handling - only line 138 has `rescue nil` while lines 143 and 146 are unprotected, risking unrescued exceptions from malformed URLs

qodo-v2-2.text: UserSerializer#include_website_name does not honor restrict_user_fields?, so TL0 users viewed anonymously can still get website_name even when website is intentionally hidden, leaking website information and potentially rendering a broken website link because the template's href still uses model.website which may be omitted while model.website_name is present

website_name compares URI(...).host (never includes port) with Discourse.current_hostname, but current_hostname can include a port suffix, causing same-domain and parent-domain checks to fail and returning only the host instead of host + path, breaking the PR's main UX goal in non-standard port setups

qodo-v22.text: UserSerializer#include_website_name includes website_name whenever website is present but does not respect Guardian#restrict_user_fields?, so TL0 users viewed anonymously can still have website host/path serialized even though website is hidden
The profile template gates display on model.website_name but uses model.website as the link target, which can produce a rendered website label with a missing/invalid href when website is restricted
website_name is not listed among untrusted_attributes so the TL0-anonymous restriction is bypassed

qodo-extended.text: UserSerializer serializes website_name for all viewers but it is not part of untrusted_attributes gating, exposing user's website host/path even when website itself is hidden under restrictions for TL0/anonymous users

UserSerializer defines include_website_name without a trailing ? so serializer inclusion conventions won't treat it as a conditional include hook, causing website_name to be emitted regardless of website presence/restrictions

website_name method fails to show full URL path when user's website is a subdomain of the Discourse instance's domain because the else branch only checks if Discourse host is a child of website host, never the reverse

website_name method parses website.to_s string into a URI object multiple times per execution instead of caching the parsed URI object once

cubic-v2.text: Method `include_website_name` is missing the `?` suffix - the serializer uses `include_<attribute>?` as its predicate convention, so without the trailing `?` this method is never called and `website_name` will be included unconditionally
The `include_website_name` method needs to apply the same `restrict_user_fields?` guard as `website`, otherwise the derived URL label can bypass the existing hidden-profile-field check

gemini.text: RSS feed content is not sanitized before being rendered as raw HTML via TopicEmbed.import, creating a critical Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability
Origin check using indexOf is insecure - discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) can be bypassed with malicious domains like discourse.com.evil.com
ALLOWALL is not a standard X-Frame-Options header value and may be ignored by browsers, leaving the page vulnerable to clickjacking
Using open from open-uri with URL from site settings can lead to SSRF or local file inclusion vulnerabilities
URL variable interpolated directly into HTML anchor tag without escaping could allow XSS via javascript: scheme URLs
Using open from open-uri with user-influenced embed_url can lead to SSRF, local file inclusion, or remote command execution
Script assumes discourse-comments element exists and will throw TypeError if it doesn't
Jobs::PollFeed.new.execute({}) is called synchronously which can cause long request times and timeouts when triggered from web requests
Page auto-reloads every 30 seconds without retry limit, causing infinite reload loop if topic retrieval fails
Using full request.referer as postMessage target origin instead of constructing origin from scheme and host is less secure

claude.text: Origin validation in embed.js uses indexOf which can be bypassed - an attacker could use origin 'http://example.com.evil.com' and indexOf would return 0, bypassing the check
X-Frame-Options set to 'ALLOWALL' is not a valid value - should be 'ALLOW-FROM' with specific origin. This disables clickjacking protection entirely
request.referer is directly interpolated without escaping in embed.html.erb, allowing potential XSS and postMessage to untrusted origins if attacker controls referer header
open() calls in poll_feed.rb and topic_embed.rb have no error handling, timeouts, or resource cleanup - can lead to hanging jobs, resource exhaustion, or unhandled exceptions

TOCTOU race condition in TopicEmbed.import - between checking if embed exists and creating it, another process could create the same embed, leading to duplicate topics or database constraint violation
URL validation in topic_embed.rb and poll_feed.rb only checks for http/https prefix but doesn't validate URL structure - could allow SSRF attacks to localhost or metadata endpoints
Host validation in topic_retriever.rb doesn't prevent SSRF to internal networks - attacker could use URL like 'http://embeddable_host@internal.server/' to bypass host check

qodo.text: XSS vulnerability: Embedded/imported content is persisted and rendered as raw HTML using cook_method raw_html and raw post.cooked. RSS/remote HTML can contain dangerous constructs like javascript: URLs, malformed attributes, data URIs, or unexpected tags. Server-side sanitization should be applied to contents before storing/rendering, and unsafe URL schemes in href/src should be validated/stripped.

SSRF vulnerability: TopicEmbed.import_remote fetches arbitrary URLs via open(url).read and Jobs::PollFeed fetches SiteSetting.feed_polling_url via open(...) without hardening. A malicious/compromised URL could target internal network resources or cause resource exhaustion.

Remote fetching uses open(url).read without explicit timeouts, size limits, redirect limits, or network allow/deny lists. This can enable SSRF to internal services, large response memory pressure, or long-hanging jobs. Should add strict timeouts, limit redirects, constrain IP ranges, and consider using a hardened HTTP client.

Clickjacking vulnerability: EmbedController sets X-Frame-Options to ALLOWALL and relies on request.referer host matching. Referer is not a reliable security boundary and can be absent/spoofed. Should use CSP frame-ancestors with explicit allowed origins and validate embedding origin robustly.
The referer host check may be too strict/too lax depending on ports/subdomains, and X-Frame-Options ALLOW-FROM should be considered where applicable.

bugbot.text: RSS feed polling crashes on items without content - code calls i.content.scrub assuming every RSS item has a content field, but many feeds use description instead or have neither field, causing NoMethodError when i.content is nil
Unused feed_key method is dead code - method is defined but never called anywhere in the codebase, appears to be leftover code for caching feed modification timestamps
RSS items without title crash topic creation - i.title is passed directly to TopicEmbed.import without checking if it's nil, and RSS/ATOM feeds can have items without titles

Weak postMessage origin check allows bypass - origin validation uses discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1 which allows bypass when attacker controls a domain that is a prefix of the legitimate domain

Missing nil check crashes on deleted embedded post - when updating an existing embed, embed.post is assigned without checking if the post still exists, causing NoMethodError when trying to revise a nil post

copilot.text: The `cook_method` column default of `1` conflicts with `Post.cook_methods = Enum.new(:regular, :raw_html)`, making new posts default to `:raw_html` and causing `Post#cook` to bypass the rendering/sanitization pipeline for regular user posts. Update the default to the enum value for `:regular` (e.g., `0`)

The `postMessage` target origin is set to the full referrer URL (`request.referer`), but browsers compare the origin (scheme/host/port) only; including the path will prevent the message from being delivered in some user agents. Build a proper origin string from the referrer using URI parsing

Method calls `open(url)` without explicitly requiring `'open-uri'`, which can cause `open` to treat the URL as a local filename if `open-uri` has not already been loaded elsewhere. Add an explicit `require 'open-uri'` before using `open(url)`

The code assumes `i.content` is always present and calls `.scrub` unconditionally, but many RSS/Atom feeds only provide `description` or other fields, causing `nil.scrub` and crashing the job. Guard against missing content with fallback to `i.description` or skip items with no textual content

augment.text: The origin check `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin)` can be bypassed by attacker-controlled origins that contain the Discourse URL as a substring (e.g., `https://discourse.example.com.evil.com`). Need stricter origin validation before trusting resize messages.

`postMessage`'s `targetOrigin` is set to `request.referer`, but browsers expect an origin (`scheme://host[:port]`), not a full URL with path/query. Passing the full referer can throw and break the iframe resize handshake.

Some feeds don't provide `content` for items; `i.content.scrub` will raise if `i.content` is `nil`, which would break polling. Need to guard/fallback to another field (e.g., description) before scrubbing/unescaping.

`url` is interpolated directly into an `<a href='...'>` inside a raw HTML string, so quotes or unexpected characters in the URL can break markup and potentially create an injection/XSS vector. Need to escape/sanitize the URL before embedding it into HTML.

When `cook_method` is `:raw_html`, `cook` returns `raw` and bypasses the normal cooking/sanitization pipeline. If any imported content isn't strictly sanitized upstream (RSS or remote fetch), this can become an XSS vector on Discourse-rendered pages.

propel.text: Using the full request.referer string as the targetOrigin breaks the postMessage call because window.parent only accepts origin strings of the form scheme://host[:port]; including the path/query from the referer means the target never matches, so the resize message is silently discarded and the iframe height never adjusts

If a feed item doesn't expose a content element (common for RSS feeds that only provide description), i.content will be nil and the line raises NoMethodError: undefined method 'scrub' for nil:NilClass, aborting the whole polling job; should fall back to description or skip empty entries before calling scrub

baz.text: SimpleRSS::Item#content is only populated for Atom <content> nodes; most RSS feeds only have <description>/<content:encoded> so i.content is nil and i.content.scrub will raise NoMethodError, causing the poll job to crash before any posts are imported. Need to fall back to description/content_encoded/summary and guard nil before scrubbing

parent.postMessage is called with request.referer which includes the full URL (path/query) instead of just the origin, so the browser rejects the targetOrigin and the resizing message never reaches the parent; should pass only the scheme+host(+port) origin

fetch_http silently returns whenever SiteSetting.embed_by_username is blank or resolves to no user, so TopicRetriever never creates an embed (the embed stays stuck on loading when the username is missing/deleted); should surface an error or require a valid username before enqueuing retrieval

EmbedController#best enqueues retrieval for any embed_url, but TopicRetriever#invalid_host? bails when embed_url host != embeddable_host, so the loader view (which reloads every 30s) never leaves the loading state for mismatched URLs; should validate the embed_url host or render an error before enqueueing to prevent infinite loop

Consider using image_tag for safer avatar rendering instead of directly interpolating URL into src attribute

sourcery.text: Using `default: 1` for `cook_method` makes all existing posts render as raw HTML, introducing a behavior change and XSS risk since `Post.cook_methods` maps `0 => :regular` and `1 => :raw_html`

Raw HTML embeds are rendered without any sanitization, which is a strong XSS risk - `import` sends remote `contents` through `absolutize_urls` and creates the post with `cook_method: Post.cook_methods[:raw_html]`, completely bypassing the cooking/sanitization pipeline
`open(url)` is used without timeouts or error handling, which can indefinitely block workers on slow/unresponsive hosts and exposes an SSRF vector if `url` is user-controlled
Assuming `i.content` is always present can raise when the feed omits it - `SimpleRSS` may not set `i.content` for all feeds, so `i.content.scrub` can raise on `nil`
In `EmbedController#best` using `params.require(:embed_url)` raises `ActionController::ParameterMissing` (400) when missing, but the spec expects a 404

cubic-dev.text: `open(url)` uses `Kernel#open`, which follows arbitrary URLs and can execute shell commands (strings starting with `|`). This enables SSRF against internal services and potential RCE. Use a dedicated HTTP library or OpenURI with URI validation and validate the URL against an allow-list of external hosts before fetching.
`end if` is a syntax error - it's missing a condition. This should be `end` to close the `if/else` block. As written, this template will fail to render.
`force: true` on `create_table` in a migration will silently drop the `top_topics` table and all its data if it already exists. This risks production data loss if the migration is ever re-run.

Unbounded reload loop: `document.location.reload()` fires every 30 seconds with no retry limit or backoff. If the embed content never becomes available, each visitor's browser will hammer the server indefinitely.
`force: true` on `create_table` in migration will silently drop and recreate the `topic_embeds` table if it already exists, destroying all data.

Insecure origin validation: `indexOf` performs a substring match, so any origin that is a prefix of `discourseUrl` will pass. An attacker origin `https://forum.example.co` would bypass the check when `discourseUrl` is `https://forum.example.com/`.

XSS / incorrect escaping: `request.referer` is output with HTML escaping inside a JavaScript string context. HTML escaping corrupts URLs containing `&` and is not the correct defense against JavaScript injection. Use `escape_javascript` for JS string contexts.

`contents` is used without a nil guard (`<<` will raise `NoMethodError` if nil), and `url` is interpolated directly into an HTML anchor tag without escaping. A crafted URL can inject arbitrary HTML/JS, leading to stored XSS.
Using `raw` to render `post.cooked` in an embed view risks XSS on the embedding site if server-side sanitization is ever bypassed. Consider wrapping in an additional sanitize pass.
`import_remote` fetches each thread URL live via `open(url).read` with no error handling. If any Disqus thread URL is unreachable, the entire import will abort with an unhandled exception.

`TopicEmbed.import_remote` does not accept or forward a `created_at` parameter, so the original Disqus thread timestamps are silently discarded. All imported topics will be dated as of the import time.
Early return bypasses the `:after_post_cook` plugin filter. Plugins that register filters on cooked content will silently skip `raw_html` posts.
Race condition: `setnx` + `expire` is not atomic. If the process dies between the two calls, the key persists with no TTL, permanently blocking retrieval for this embed URL.
The return value of `@post.save(...)` is not checked. If validation fails, subsequent operations still execute on an unsaved post, and `revise!` returns `true`.
Wrong file required in spec. This loads `ProcessPost` instead of `PollFeed`. Should be `require 'jobs/scheduled/poll_feed'`.
`i.content` can be nil for RSS items that lack a `content` element. Calling `.scrub` on nil will raise `NoMethodError`, crashing the job.
Using `open()` with a configurable URL is vulnerable to command injection. `Kernel#open` treats strings starting with `|` as shell pipe commands. Use `OpenURI.open_uri` instead.

The `embed_url` parameter is used without URL format validation. It's eventually fetched by `TopicRetriever` which calls `open(url).read`. Without verifying the URL scheme is `http`/`https` and the host is not an internal address, this is an SSRF vector.
Add rel="noopener" to target="_blank" links to prevent opener access.
Assertions in `after` block is an anti-pattern. If an example raises before reaching `get :best`, the `after` block still runs and fails on the response check, masking the real error.
No null check on `comments`. If the `#discourse-comments` element is absent from the page, `comments.appendChild(iframe)` will throw a `TypeError`.
`start_with?(...).should be_true` is a weak assertion that hides both partial matches and diff details on failure. Use `should ==` for an exact comparison.
Test passes for the wrong reason. `feed_polling_enabled?` is not stubbed to `true`, so the AND condition short-circuits before `feed_polling_url` is ever evaluated.
Same issue: `feed_polling_enabled?` and `feed_polling_url` are not stubbed to truthy values, so test passes because earlier AND conditions fail.
`feed_key` is defined but never called - this is dead code.
Spec expects `TopicRetriever` to be called, but the controller enqueues `Jobs.enqueue(:retrieve_topic, ...)` instead.
`ALLOWALL` is not a valid `X-Frame-Options` value per RFC 7034. Some browsers treat unrecognized values as `DENY`, which would prevent the embed from loading.
Guard against a nil/empty RSS parse result before iterating to avoid a NoMethodError when the feed fails to parse.
Guard against a blank referrer before calling postMessage; an empty targetOrigin will throw and break the resize messaging.
Missing newline at end of file in embed.css.scss.

claude-code.text: XSS vulnerability: the `url` parameter on line 13 is interpolated directly into HTML without escaping. Since the href uses single quotes, a URL like `http://evil.com/'><script>alert(1)</script>` breaks out of the attribute and injects script tags. Combined with `cook_method: raw_html` (bypassing sanitization) and `raw post.cooked` in the embed view, this allows arbitrary JavaScript execution. The URL should be HTML-escaped with `ERB::Util.html_escape` or `CGI.escapeHTML` before interpolation.

ERB syntax error on line 6: `<%- end if %>` should be `<%- end %>`. In Ruby, `end if` is a postfix conditional modifier that requires a condition expression, but none is provided here. This will raise a `SyntaxError` when rendering the embed view, completely breaking the embeddable comments feature for any topic with posts.

On line 35, `i.content` returns `nil` for RSS 2.0 feeds that use `<description>` instead of `<content>`, causing `NoMethodError` when `.scrub` is called on `nil`. Since the job has `sidekiq_options retry: false`, this crashes the entire feed polling job with no retry. Should fall back to `i.description` and guard against nil.

The origin check `discourseUrl.indexOf(e.origin) === -1` on line 17 uses substring matching, which allows any origin that is a prefix/substring of `discourseUrl` to pass validation. Should use strict origin comparison: `new URL(discourseUrl).origin === e.origin`.

bugbot.text: Two `downsize` methods are defined with the same name—one taking `max_width, max_height` arguments (line 145) and another taking a single `dimensions` argument (line 149). In Ruby, the second definition overwrites the first. The existing caller in `resize_emoji.rb` uses `OptimizedImage.downsize(path, path, 100, 100, opts)` with 4 positional arguments, which will now fail with an `ArgumentError` since only the 3-argument version exists.

The client-side file size validation now uses a hardcoded 10MB limit for all file types instead of the type-specific `max_{type}_size_kb` site setting. While this works for images (which get auto-downsized server-side), attachments have no such downsizing. Users can select attachments larger than `max_attachment_size_kb`, the client accepts them, but the server rejects them during upload validation.

The auto-downsize code passes `tempfile.path` to `OptimizedImage.downsize`, but `tempfile.path` is a system-generated path without the original file extension. The animated GIF detection in `optimize()` relies on matching the path against `/\.GIF$/i`, which will never match. As a result, animated GIFs are processed with `downsize_instructions` which uses `[0]` to extract only the first frame, permanently destroying all animation frames.

Client-side validation now enforces a hardcoded 10MB max for uploads, ignoring SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb/max_attachment_size_kb that the server actually enforces. This causes incorrect client rejections when settings allow larger uploads and misleading client acceptance (followed by server rejection) when settings allow smaller uploads.

UploadsController#create_upload now caps API URL downloads at a fixed 10MB instead of respecting SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb. This breaks configured deployments that allow >10MB images (downloads fail early) and creates inconsistent limits compared to other URL-download paths that still use SiteSetting.max_image_size_kb.

qodo-v22.text: OptimizedImage.downsize is defined twice, and the later 3-arg+opts definition overwrites the original width/height signature, so existing callers passing (from,to,width,height,opts) will raise ArgumentError. This breaks Jobs::ResizeEmoji at runtime and prevents emoji resizing from running.

For API URL uploads, the server now downloads up to a fixed 10MB regardless of configured max image/attachment sizes, wasting bandwidth/CPU for attachments that will be rejected and preventing downsizing for images >10MB because they never download. This changes behavior from being setting-driven to hardcoded and can be abused to force repeated 10MB downloads.

Server-side URL uploads in UploadsController are capped at 10MB regardless of configuration, conflicting with configurable SiteSettings

UploadsController#create_upload loops up to 5 downsizing attempts but never checks whether the image conversion succeeded, so failures silently continue and the upload may still exceed max_image_size_kb

The PR adds a new def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={}) at line 149 without removing the old 4-arg version at line 145, causing Ruby to use last definition and breaking existing caller in resize_emoji.rb which passes 5 arguments

After OptimizedImage.downsize invokes an external convert process that overwrites the file at tempfile.path, tempfile.size uses fstat on the original file descriptor which may return stale size if convert replaces the file with a new inode, causing the loop to always run all 5 iterations and Upload.create_for to record incorrect file size metadata

cubic-v2.text: Second `downsize` definition overrides the width/height version, so existing callers that pass separate max width and height will start raising `ArgumentError`
Upload validation hardcodes 10 MB and ignores the configured image/attachment size limits from Discourse.SiteSettings

qodo-extended-summary.text: The downsizing loop ignores the boolean result of OptimizedImage.downsize, so conversion failures will still retry up to 5 times and then proceed to Upload.create_for with an oversized/invalid file instead of failing fast with a clear message

The PR adds a new def self.downsize(from, to, dimensions, opts={}) without removing the existing 4-arg def self.downsize(from, to, max_width, max_height, opts={}). In Ruby there is no method overloading so the second definition silently replaces the first, breaking the existing caller in resize_emoji.rb which passes 5 arguments

The dynamic Discourse.SiteSettings['max_' + type + '_size_kb'] is replaced with a hardcoded 10MB for all upload types, not just images, disabling admin-configurable client-side file size validation for attachments and creating inconsistency with server-side limits

The while-loop evaluates tempfile.size to check if downsizing was successful, but tempfile is an open Ruby file descriptor pointing to the original deleted file because ImageMagick's convert replaces the file on disk. The loop will use stale size data and Upload.create_for will use the original large file

API URL uploads are now downloaded with a hardcoded 10MB cap instead of using the site's configured limits, which can unnecessarily consume bandwidth/IO and expands the amount of data an attacker can force the server to download per request before validation

qodo.text: The new topics#unsubscribe endpoint is reachable via GET and changes server-side state (topic notification level). If CSRF protections are not enforced, a malicious site could cause a logged-in user's browser to hit the URL and alter preferences unintentionally, and email scanners/link prefetchers could also trigger it. Consider requiring a non-GET method with CSRF token or adding a confirmation page/tokenized link.

The unsubscribe action assumes a TopicUser row exists and that current_user is present. If the topic has never been visited (no TopicUser row) or the user is not logged in, tu/current_user can be nil and tu.notification_level / current_user.id will raise. Consider using TopicUser.change (which upserts), or guard/redirect when unauthenticated, and handle nil TopicUser safely.

Unsubscribe is implemented as a GET route that mutates state (toggles notification level). This is surprising from an HTTP semantics perspective and makes it easier to trigger unintended changes (e.g., link prefetching/crawlers). Consider making the state change a POST/PUT with CSRF protection, while still allowing a GET landing page that requires an explicit user action to confirm.
The computed property name stopNotificiationsText is misspelled (should be stopNotificationsText). This typo increases maintenance cost and can confuse future contributors.

entelligence.text: TopicUser.find_by will return nil if the user has no existing record for this topic, causing a NoMethodError on the next line. Need to use find_or_initialize_by(user_id: current_user.id, topic_id: params[:topic_id]) or add a nil check to handle users who haven't interacted with the topic yet.

sourcery.text: TopicUser.find_by may return nil when the user's first interaction with the topic is via email, causing tu.notification_level to raise. Handle the nil case explicitly (e.g., create a TopicUser with the correct level or return/redirect safely) so the unsubscribe action doesn't error for these users.

unsubscribe_url currently appends /unsubscribe to url, which is usually relative (e.g. /t/slug/123). This is interpolated directly into the email copy via %{unsubscribe_url}, so the email will contain a relative link that may not work in many mail clients. Build an absolute URL using Discourse.base_url or an existing helper.

Clearing details.notificationReasonText on the shared topic model means that if the user returns to the normal topic view in the same session, the notification reason will still be null and never reappear. Either restore the original value on deactivate/willTransition, or avoid mutating the model and use a route/controller-level flag to hide the text in the template.

The class attribute is set to .previous-discussion, so the element's class name includes the dot and won't match the .previous-discussion CSS selector. Change it to class='previous-discussion' so the selector applies correctly.

In app/views/email/notification.html.erb the %{respond_instructions} footer is now rendered both inside the context_posts.present? block and again after the <hr>, which will duplicate the instructions when context_posts are present; consider only rendering it once.

The TopicUnsubscribe client route is only defined for /t/:slug/:id/unsubscribe, but the server also exposes /t/:topic_id/unsubscribe; if users can hit the slugless URL directly in the browser, consider adding a matching Ember route or always redirecting to the slugged form to avoid inconsistent client behavior.

cubic-dev.text: `TopicUser.find_by` can return `nil` if the user has no record for this topic, causing a `NoMethodError` on the next line. Add a nil guard or use `find_or_create_by` to handle the case where no `TopicUser` record exists.

The unsubscribe route uses GET but the controller action mutates state (`tu.save!`). GET requests must be idempotent per HTTP semantics. Since these links will appear in emails, email security scanners, link prefetchers, and anti-spam crawlers commonly follow GET links automatically — this would silently unsubscribe users without their intent. The GET route should render a confirmation page, and the actual state change should happen via a POST/PUT.

Bug: `user_id` is assigned `topic` instead of `user` in the fallback branch two lines above. When `track_visit!` is called with raw integer IDs (as it is from `TopicsController`), `user_id` receives the topic ID, so this query updates the wrong user's visit record. The fix is on the `user_id = …` line: change `… : topic` to `… : user`.
The `title` is concatenated directly into HTML without escaping, which is an XSS risk. Use `Handlebars.Utils.escapeExpression(title)` to sanitize the value before inserting it into the buffer.

The HTML class value `.previous-discussion` includes a leading dot, which is CSS selector syntax and should not appear in an HTML `class` attribute. This prevents CSS rules targeting `.previous-discussion` from matching this element.
`bottom: initial` is not supported in IE. Use `bottom: auto` instead — it's equivalent (since `auto` is the initial value of `bottom`) and has universal browser support.

Typo in property name: `stopNotificiationsText` should be `stopNotificationsText` (extra 'i' in "Notificiations"). The matching template also has the same misspelling, so both should be corrected together.

The shared `unsubscribe_link` locale now requires `%{unsubscribe_url}`, which is not part of the default `template_args` in `MessageBuilder`. Unlike `user_preferences_url` (always initialized), `unsubscribe_url` is only provided by the topic-notification caller. If `add_unsubscribe_link` is ever set to true without passing `unsubscribe_url`, this will raise `I18n::MissingInterpolationArgument`. Consider either adding a default `unsubscribe_url` in `MessageBuilder#initialize` or splitting the topic-specific unsubscribe text into a separate locale key.

Use an arrow function instead of `const self = this`. The rest of this diff already adopts ES6 features (shorthand methods, `const`); the `self` capture pattern is the one leftover that arrow functions make unnecessary.

Inconsistent trailing punctuation: `stop_notifications` ends with a period but `change_notification_state` does not. Since both strings are in the same `unsubscribe` block and likely displayed together, the missing period is a visible inconsistency.

augment.text: dayjs(date.start) === dayjs(date.end) compares object identity, so this condition will always be false and the zero-length override detection branch will never run - needs to use isSame or timestamp compare instead
